The VLC media player has returned to Apple mobile devices after more than two years of absence, The Next Web reports. The program is expected to appear in the App Store on Friday, July 19. The player is free and can play many video and audio formats. Its features include support for network streams and AirPlay technology, subtitles, and integration with the Dropbox cloud file storage. The application is compatible with iPhone smartphones, iPod touch players and iPad tablets running iOS 5.1 and later versions of the operating system. The first version of VLC was released on iOS in the fall of 2010, but in January 2011 the player was removed from the App Store by agreement between Apple and the project's development team. The reason for VLC's removal from the store was a disagreement over licensing: the rules of the App Store conflicted with the terms of the "free" GNU GPL license under which the player was distributed. Now the developers of the player have eliminated the contradictions by publishing the VLC code under two licenses at once: GNU GPL Version 2 and Mozilla Public License Version 2. The latter allows the distribution of the player through the App Store. VLC player is available on Windows, OS X, Linux and other platforms. In the summer of 2012, the player was released on Android, but it still has the status of a beta version.

Scientists have found that some species of parasitic wasps are unable to produce fertile offspring only because their intestines contain different symbiotic bacteria. This, according to biologists, suggests that symbionts can influence the formation of new species in the same way that geographic isolation does. The study was published in the journal Science, and its summary is provided by ScienceNow. The scientists' conclusions are based on work with three species of parasitic wasps that lay their eggs in fly larvae. Two of the three species, Nasonia giraulti and Nasonia longicornis, are evolutionarily quite close and can easily form fertile hybrids. The third species, Nasonia vitripennis, separated from the others about a million years ago, and crossing any of the first two species with it results in the formation of offspring that quickly die. Having discovered that two closely related species, unlike a third, have almost identical microflora, scientists decided to test its effect on crossing. To do this, the wasps were raised on sterile feed with antibiotics and then crossed with each other. It turned out that almost all bacteria-free hybrids with a "distant" species (Nasonia vitripennis) in this case begin to survive. If microbiologically sterile insects are infected with bacteria, their offspring again become non-viable. Scientists do not yet know what mechanisms cause the death of interspecies hybrids. Presumably, such insects have an altered immune system that is not suitable for controlling "foreign" symbiotic bacteria. The work of biologists is important for understanding the mechanisms of speciation. Among the known "classical" mechanisms of species separation are geographical and ecological isolation. Also, it has recently been experimentally shown that sexual selection alone is sometimes sufficient to maintain species separation.

Road signs have appeared on highways in the San Francisco Bay Area in California informing drivers that speed is being monitored by unmanned aerial vehicles, KPIX 5 reports. The road sign, in addition to the notice, had an image of a drone. Next to the aircraft, an object was drawn that vaguely resembled a rocket. The local traffic police called the signs fake and said they were installed illegally. The police assured that they do not have drones on their balance sheet, especially with weapons on board, and that speed is measured by other methods. At the same time, the road patrol service noted the high quality of the counterfeits. The signs were made in compliance with all requirements, they had a reflective coating, and one of the signs was installed using vandal-proof fasteners. The signs were dismantled; now the police are trying to find the people who installed them on the road. It is not reported how many signs the pranksters put up in total. It should be noted that the California Highway Patrol uses aircraft with pilots on board to monitor the traffic situation. Drivers are warned about this by the corresponding signs. In a number of countries, drones are also used to monitor the road - such an experiment, in particular, has started in Russia, in the Krasnoyarsk Territory.

Google has released a new version of the Maps app for iOS, the official blog reports. Now the program supports not only iPhone and iPod touch, but also iPad tablets. Other new features in the app include the “Search Nearby” function, which displays places of interest to the user in the surrounding area, navigation inside buildings (shopping malls, train stations and airports are now available) and the ability to re-route when the traffic situation changes. Some of the new features, such as the traffic incident reporting service, integration with the Zagat restaurant guide, and display of special offers from stores and cafes, only work in the United States. The program has the ability to save the map in the cache. It allows you to check Google Maps without Internet access. To save the map, you need to open the desired fragment on the screen and type the phrase "ok maps" in the search bar. In Russia, however, the function does not work yet. Google Maps were used in the first versions of Apple's mobile operating system as standard maps, but in iOS 6, Tim Cook's company abandoned them in favor of its own maps. After that, Google had to distribute the program only through the App Store. The version of Google Maps that was available in the App Store until today was not designed for the large screens of tablets. Along with the iOS maps update, Google opened access to the new interface of the web version of Maps to all users. The interface was shown in May at the I/O conference, and until July 16, only those who had an invitation could work with it.

Scientists from Hungary and Great Britain have created an electric scalpel that can analyze the nature of the tissue being cut. Mass spectrometric research in real time allows the doctor to determine where cancer cells end and healthy tissue begins. This allows for more precise tumor removal. Science Now provides details with reference to the scientists' article in Science Translational Medicine. The researchers first collected 3,000 samples of healthy and cancerous tissue. They then touched them one by one with an electric scalpel and collected a sample of the gas (smoke) released. The doctors analyzed the gas mixture with a mass spectrometer, after which the results of the chemical analysis were compared with the histological examination data of the same sample. This comparison made it possible to identify unique chemical markers of different tumors and determine them by the composition of aerosols released during cutting with an electric scalpel. The instrument for performing operations was supplemented with a tube that sucks the gas mixture to the mass spectrometer and due to this, it was possible to analyze without doctors performing any additional actions during the operation. The development was tested in a clinic during operations on 81 patients, but the long-term consequences are still unknown. A conclusion on whether the new method helps to avoid tumor metastasis can only be made several years after the operations. Histological examination of tissue is currently the main and most reliable way to identify cancerous tumors: cells are examined under a microscope by an expert histologist. But when performing operations, surgeons have to wait at least half an hour to find out the nature of the removed sample. This leads to long-term anesthesia and does not provide high accuracy, which is especially important in brain operations.

Israeli engineers have developed flat and flexible sensors based on carbon nanoparticles. This development should help in creating prostheses capable of transmitting to the owner not only the sensations of touch, but also temperature and humidity. Science Daily reports on the work of specialists from the Technion-Israel Institute, citing the researchers' publication in the journal Applied Materials 'n Interfaces. The declared characteristics of the sensors are as follows: they are capable of registering mechanical loads of several hundredths of a gram, feeling temperature changes within one degree, and reacting to relative humidity fluctuations of up to one percent. In terms of mechanical sensitivity, the new development still loses out to human skin, but it is already sufficient for many practical tasks. The sensors are made of thin layers of bonded gold nanoparticles with a diameter of only 5 to 8 nanometers. Preliminary tests have shown that they remain functional when repeatedly bent together with a rubber base, and can also be placed on different materials. In addition to prosthetics, the new sensors can be used to track deformations in building structures, as well as machine components: cracks will be detected with the same success as bending or twisting of a part. The principle of operation of the sensors is based on the fact that an increase in humidity or temperature increases the conductivity of the tape of nanoparticles and the polymer substrate that binds them, and bending leads to a decrease in the distance between the nanoparticles with a change in electrical resistance. Previously, scientists could make flat and flexible pressure sensors, but they could not register temperature and humidity.

The Russian Ministry of Defense will soon acquire new Russian-made mobile hospitals based on the airmobile hospitals of the Tsentrospas detachment of the Ministry of Emergency Situations. As the Izvestia newspaper writes, citing a source in the Main Military Medical Directorate, requirements for the equipment of the new hospitals are currently being developed, taking into account scenarios for their use in combat situations. In total, it is planned to buy four mobile hospitals; one for each military district. Previously, military doctors planned to buy hospitals from the German company Zeppelin. Now, however, a decision has been made to buy only Russian-made hospitals. Moreover, in the military version, special attention will be paid to equipping them with surgical instruments, equipment, and consumables. Currently, the special purpose medical units of the Russian Armed Forces use mobile medical complexes based on KamAZ vehicles. The capacity of these vehicles is up to 250 people; the complexes are equipped with a full range of medical equipment and are not inferior in their capabilities to stationary military hospitals. However, the deployment time of medical centers based on KamAZ vehicles is too long. The airmobile hospital, which is in service with the Russian Emergencies Ministry, includes resuscitation, operating, consultative and diagnostic, and X-ray units, an ultrasound diagnostic and electrocardiography unit, and a blood laboratory. The hospital can accept up to one hundred people per day and operate autonomously for two weeks. The complex can be delivered to any point by plane and deployed in a short time.

The English Football Championship silver medalist Manchester City has bought Spanish national team striker Alvaro Negredo from Sevilla. This was reported on the English club's official website. According to the online publication Goal.com, the forward's transfer value was 21.7 million pounds (25.2 million euros), and Sevilla could receive up to 2.6 million pounds (three million euros) depending on the 27-year-old player's performance. Negredo became Manchester City's third acquisition in the summer of 2013. The English club had previously bought Spanish midfielder Jesus Navas from Sevilla and Brazilian midfielder Fernandinho from Shakhtar Donetsk. Manchester City could also be joined by Italian Fiorentina forward Stevan Jovetic. Negredo began his professional career at Rayo Vallecano, then moved to Real Madrid, but only played for the team's reserves. In 2007, Negredo moved to Almeria, and since 2009 he has played for Sevilla. In the 2012/13 Spanish Championship, the forward played 36 matches and scored 25 goals. Negredo has been playing for the Spanish national team since 2009. He has played 14 matches and scored six goals for the national team. In 2012, Negredo became European champion with his team.

The International Football Federation (FIFA) has announced ticket prices for matches of the World Cup, which will be held in 2014 in Brazil, Associated Press reports. The cheapest ticket for foreign spectators will cost $90. It will allow fans who have come to Brazil from abroad to attend one of the matches of the group stage of the competition. For a seat in the stands during the final match, foreigners will have to pay from $440 to $990. For residents of Brazil, the cheapest ticket to the home World Cup will cost $15. This is the amount that Brazilian students, residents of the country over 60 years old and participants in a number of social programs will need to attend a group stage match. The minimum ticket price for other residents of the country starts at $30. At the previous World Cup, held in 2010 in South Africa, the minimum ticket price for local residents was $20. Earlier, the Brazilian authorities promised that tickets for the 2014 World Cup for the country's residents would be the cheapest in the history of the competition. However, AP notes, most residents of Brazil, where the official minimum wage is about $340, will not be able to afford even discounted tickets. The first stage of ticket sales for the 2014 World Cup will take place on the FIFA website from August 20 to October 10. Ticket holders for the most popular matches will be determined by a random selection of applications submitted. The second stage of ticket sales will begin on December 8, after the schedule of group stage matches is announced. In total, the tournament organizers plan to sell about three million tickets. At least 400 thousand of them are reserved for residents of Brazil, including about 50 thousand tickets for those who worked on the construction of stadiums and other infrastructure for the World Cup. The exact number of tickets that will go on sale will be determined after all 12 stadiums hosting the 2014 World Cup matches are completed, FIFA emphasized. Brazil's budget expenditure on the preparation and hosting of the 2014 World Cup will amount, according to preliminary estimates, to about 14 billion euros. The costs of the World Cup became one of the reasons for the mass protests that took place in Brazil in late May - early June 2013.

Forbes magazine has named Robert Downey Jr. the highest paid actor in Hollywood. The publication writes that, according to its information, the actor earned about $75 million from June 2012 to June 2013. Forbes notes that during this period, two films with Downey Jr. were released, each of which grossed more than a billion dollars worldwide. We are talking about "The Avengers" and "Iron Man 2", in which the actor played the role of billionaire and superhero Tony Stark. Second place in the Forbes top was taken by Channing Tatum, who earned approximately $60 million in a year thanks to the film Magic Mike. Third place went to Hugh Jackman, whose latest work is the musical film Les Miserables. The Australian actor earned five million less than Tatum. Mark Wahlberg came in fourth (as Forbes notes, the success of the film Ted is to blame for this. The film grossed over $550 million at the box office). Former wrestler Dwayne "The Rock" Johnson, who starred in the sixth installment of Fast and Furious and in G.I. Joe: Retaliation, came in fifth. The rating also includes Leonardo DiCaprio, Adam Sandler, Tom Cruise, Denzel Washington and Liam Neeson. As Forbes notes, Cruise, who topped the top ten a year ago, this time took only eighth place in the list of the highest-paid actors in Hollywood.

The American Television Academy announced the Emmy nominees on Thursday, July 18. The full list of nominees is available on the awards website. The nominees for the award in the category of "Best Drama Series" were "Breaking Bad", "Downton Abbey", "Game Of Thrones", "Homeland", "Mad Men" and "House of Cards". The contenders for the title of best comedy series will be 30 Rock, Girls, Louie, Modern Family, Big Bang Theory and Veep. The nominees for Best Limited Series or Television Film are American Horror Story, Behind the Candelabra, Phil Spector, Political Animals, The Bible and Top of the Lake. The 65th Emmy Awards will take place in Los Angeles on September 22. Actor Neil Patrick Harris will host the ceremony.

The Sven Co-op modification to the Half-Life game will become a separate game. This was announced on the official forum of the project by its developers. According to the team working on the mod, this became possible thanks to cooperation with the Valve studio, which released Half-Life. The company provided the creators of the mod with access to the GoldSrc engine, on the basis of which the developers will make an independent version of their modification. After the work is completed, the game will be released on the Steam service, where it will be available to all its users for free. It will include the entire Half-Life 1 campaign, and gamers will not have to buy this shooter separately in order to play Sven Co-op. In the future, the developers also promised that they will continue to support the project and will regularly release updates to it. However, they did not say when exactly the standalone version of Sven Co-op will be released. Work on Sven Co-op has been going on for the last 15 years. The first version of the mod appeared in 1999. Thus, it is considered the oldest Half-Life mod that is still in development. However, as the creators of the mod note, they are currently preparing to release its final version, after which they will begin creating a separate game. It is worth noting that earlier gamers released another fan project on the first part of Half-Life. In September 2012, the game Black Mesa was released, which is a significantly improved version of the shooter. Black Mesa is made on the basis of the Source engine. It is worth noting, however, that this game includes only a part of the game. The adventures of Gordon Freeman in the world of Xen in Black Mesa are not included, and the developers of the shooter plan to release them later. The first Half-Life game went on sale in 1998. Six years later, Valve released a sequel to the game, and then introduced two spin-offs of the series that take place in the same world as HL — Portal and Portal 2. The third part of Gordon Freeman's story is considered one of the most anticipated games in the world. Rumors about its upcoming announcement regularly appear on the Internet, but they have never been officially confirmed.

Publisher Ubisoft has clarified the release date of the post-apocalyptic MMO The Division. As Eurogamer reports, citing company representatives, the game will go on sale at the end of 2014. It was previously known that it would be released next year, but a more precise date was not named. At a meeting with investors, the head of the company, Yves Guillemot, also specified the release date of the racing game The Crew. According to him, it should go on sale before April 1, 2014. In addition, Ubisoft announced the postponement of its as yet unannounced franchise. Now it will go on sale only in the next financial year (that is, between April 2014 and the end of March 2015). The head of the company did not provide any details about this project. The multiplayer game The Division was presented at the E3-2013 exhibition. It is scheduled to be released on the next-generation consoles Xbox One and PS4. The game is being developed by Massive Entertainment, known for Far Cry 3 and Assassin's Creed Revelations. The Division takes place in an alternative version of our world, where one day on Black Friday, an extremely dangerous virus begins to spread around the world. In just five days, it leads to a global catastrophe. The game takes place shortly after the epidemic. Its heroes are employees of a special department, whose goal is to save the surviving population. After it became known that Ubisoft was planning to release the game only on consoles, gamers demanded that the publisher also make a PC version of the project. They even started collecting signatures for a corresponding petition (and collected more than 131 thousand). However, the publisher has not yet given a clear answer to the question of whether the game will be released on this platform..

The lead singer of the group "King and the Jester" Mikhail Gorshenev, nicknamed Gorshok, died on the night of July 19, as reported on the group's Facebook page. There is no word yet on the musician's cause of death. The report only says that Gorshenev died at his home in St. Petersburg and that other details will be published later. The group also clarified that all announced performances of "The King and the Jester" are cancelled. Meanwhile, the group's next concert is scheduled for July 20 at the Green Theater of Gorky Park. Gorshenev was born on August 7, 1973, in Boksitogorsk, Leningrad Oblast. He began studying music while still in school. In 1988, together with classmates Alexander Balunov and Alexander Shchigolev, he founded the band "Kontora". In 1990, Andrey Knyazev joined them. That same year, they named their band "King of Jesters", which later received its current name. The musicians released their first studio album, "Kamnem po golovom", in 1996. In total, the group has recorded 12 studio albums throughout its history. Gorshenev was also one of the authors of the idea of the musical "TODD", dedicated to the hairdresser Sweeney Todd. The musician played the main role in the production. In 2011 and 2012, based on the materials of the musical "The King and the Jester", he released two albums. The musician was married twice. His second wife's name is Olga. In 2009, she gave birth to Gorshenev's daughter. In an interview with the authors of the book "And the Living Will Sing About the Dead," dedicated to the group "King and the Jester," Gorshenev admitted that he used heroin in the 1990s. According to the musician, he experienced clinical death eight times due to overdoses. The book was published in 2007.

London's Globe Theatre will go on tour for two years in 2014 to mark the 450th anniversary of Shakespeare's birth. The Globe's website reports that the theatre will be touring the world with a production of Hamlet. This tour will be the first of such scale in the history of the theatre. The Globe troupe is planning to visit all countries in the world as part of the touring marathon and perform in theatres, on beaches, jungles and castles. The theatre’s task is to give the performance in 205 countries. The last point on the tour will be the Danish city of Helsingor (Elsinore), where the action of Hamlet takes place. The theatre's artistic director Dominic Dromgoole explained the choice of the production by the fact that Hamlet is a play by Shakespeare that is known everywhere. The theatre's choice was approved by theatre director Peter Brook, who noted that the simplest words in the English language, "to be or not to be", are from Hamlet and there is hardly a single place on the planet where they have not been translated. The theatre marathon will begin on 23 April 2014 at the Globe Theatre and will end there exactly two years later, in 2016. That day will also be Dromgoole’s last as artistic director of the theatre. The Globe Theatre was built in London in 1599 with funds from the acting troupe of the Lord Chamberlain's Men, to which Shakespeare belonged. In 1613, the theatre burned down, then it was rebuilt and in this form it existed until 1642. The modern Globe was built in 1997 not far from the original site of the theatre. The Globe regularly holds large-scale projects, trying to unite people of different cultures and nationalities through the work of the great English playwright. Thus, in the spring of 2012, before the Olympic Games in London, the Globe held a festival at which 37 troupes performed and presented plays based on Shakespeare's works in 37 languages.

A monument to Dagestani poet Rasul Gamzatov has been unveiled in the center of Moscow. As RIA Novosti reports, the grand opening ceremony of the monument took place on Yauzsky Boulevard. It was timed to coincide with the 90th anniversary of the poet's birthday. The ceremony was attended by President Vladimir Putin, Acting Moscow Mayor Sergei Sobyanin, as well as Senator of the Federation Council from Dagestan Ilyas Ukhmanov and Acting Head of the Republic Ramazan Abdulatipov. In addition, the opening of the monument was attended by Gamzatov's relatives and friends. The authors of the monument were several sculptors, including Alexey Tikhonov, Igor Novikov and Shamil Kanaigadzhiev. The monument was erected with funds donated by the Rasul Gamzatov Foundation and donated to Moscow. Poet Rasul Gamzatov was born in 1923. He published his first collection of poems, written in the Avar language, at the age of 20. In 1952, the poet received the Stalin Prize, and 11 years later he was awarded the Lenin Prize. Among his most famous collections of poems and verses are "My Heart is in the Mountains" and "High Stars". Rasul Gamzatov died in 2003. He is buried in the village of Tarki, which is a suburb of Makhachkala.

An Italian court has sentenced five employees of Costa Crociere, the company that owns the Costa Concordia, in the case of the plane crash. They were given prison terms ranging from 1 year and 8 months to 2 years and 10 months for manslaughter, the Associated Press reported Saturday. All five had previously pleaded guilty. The Costa Crociere's crisis coordinator received the longest sentence, the helmsman and two crew officers the shortest. The ship's accommodation manager was also among those convicted. The ship's captain, Francesco Schettino, is being tried separately.

Police have conducted searches in apartments, offices and prison cells in northern Germany, the Netherlands and Switzerland in connection with the case of the organization of the international neo-Nazi terrorist group "Werwolf", Bloomberg reports, citing a statement from the German prosecutor's office. In Germany alone, about 50 police officers took part in the searches. Swiss authorities searched the cells of two prisoners who were allegedly involved in the organization. So far, six people are suspected in the case, none of whom have been arrested, Spiegel writes. Their nationalities are not specified. Law enforcement agencies claim that they have failed to uncover the plans for attacks that, according to them, the group members were hatching. The German police hope that the searches will help the investigation learn about the intentions of the suspects. At the moment, all that is known is that the group members wanted to change the system of government in Germany. In order to communicate with each other, they developed a special encryption program, notes Associated Press. The name "Werwolf" (which means werewolf in German) was borne by a Nazi militia created in 1944, when World War II was already drawing to a close. This German unit was supposed to wage guerrilla warfare behind enemy lines. German authorities have become particularly attentive to neo-Nazi movements since the NSU (National Socialist Underground) group was uncovered in 2011. Its members are accused of involvement in the murders of nine immigrants from Turkey and Greece, as well as a local policewoman. They are also accused of two bombings and numerous bank robberies. The NSU trial began in May 2013.

The gas mask consists of a face piece and a filter-absorbing system (FAS), which are connected to each other directly or with a connecting tube. The gas mask kit includes a bag and anti-fog films, and, depending on the type of gas mask, may include intercom membranes, a knitted hydrophobic cover, overhead insulating cuffs, a waterproof bag, a flask lid with a valve and a tag.

The EO-16 filtering and absorption box has the shape of a cylinder with a height of 17.5 cm and a diameter of 10.7 cm. There is an internal screwed neck in the bottom of the body. The marking on the FGS is applied with waterproof mastic on the cylindrical part of the body: the first line is the FPK-EO-16 index; the second line is the manufacturer's symbol, month, last two digits of the year of manufacture, batch number; the third line is the FPK series and number. The SHM-41Mu helmet-mask consists of a body, a goggle unit, fairings and a valve box. The SHMS helmet-mask consists of a body, a goggle unit, fairings, a valve box and a collapsible intercom. The frontal location and dimensions of the goggle unit glasses provide the ability to work with optical devices. The marking on the face parts of the SHM-41Mu and SHMS is applied in the form of a raised imprint from the mold: in the chin part, in a circle, the height of the helmet-mask is indicated by a number, the last two digits of the year of manufacture, the quarter (in dots), the conventional designation of the manufacturer (in letters), and the mold number. The bag has the shape of a rectangular parallelepiped. It is made of single-layer fabric. The valve with a strap at the end is fastened with a buckle. Two compartments of the bag are designed for a helmet-mask, a respirator and a personal protective kit (PPE). The compartment for the PPE has a neck tightened with tape. For free access of air to the PPE opening, belt loops are sewn on the bottom of the bag, into which wooden inserts are inserted. On the partition there is a buttoned pocket for boxes with anti-fog films and membranes, and there may also be an outer pocket for an individual anti-chemical package (IPP).

Gas mask PMK-3, FPK manufactured in 2006. Latest generation gas mask. New. Included: FPK KB-2V, face part MB-2, bag in flora, water intake connection device, adapter for threaded filters, hydrophobic knitted cover, grille, plug, waterproof bag, rubber rings, tag. Designed to protect the respiratory system, face and eyes from poisonous substances, toxic chemicals, biological aerosols and radioactive dust, SDYAV, SIYAV, Technical specifications: filter resource - 240 hours, duration of continuous stay - 24 hours, speech intelligibility - 95%, audibility - 100%, weight of the gas mask without a bag - 960g.

Gas mask PMG-2. Filtering and absorbing box E0-62K has the shape of a cylinder 8 cm high and 11.2 cm in diameter. Marking on the FPK is applied with waterproof mastic to the cylindrical part of the body: the first line is the FPK index - E0-62K, the second line is the batch number, series and FPK number. On the protective screen (under the plug) in the form of a convex stamp are indicated: numbers in a circle - the conventional designation of the manufacturer, the month and the last two digits of the year of manufacture. The SHM-66Mu helmet-mask consists of a body, a goggle unit, fairings, a valve box and a collapsible intercom. The face part has through cutouts for the ears, which ensures normal audibility. The marking on the SHM-66Mu corresponds to the marking on the SHM-41Mu. The size range and marking of the SHM-66Mu are similar to the range and marking of the SHM-41Mu. The bag of the PMG-2 gas mask is similar to the bag of the PMG gas mask.

The isolating gas mask IP-4 (IP-4M, IP-4 MK, IP-4 MR), unlike the filtering ones, completely isolates the respiratory organs from the environment. Breathing in the IP-4 gas mask is performed due to the oxygen supply located in the gas mask itself. The isolating gas masks IP-4, IP-4M, IP-4 MK, IP-4 MR are used when it is impossible to use filtering gas masks, in particular, when there is a lack of oxygen in the environment, at very high concentrations of toxic substances, toxic substances and other harmful substances, when working underwater. The isolating gas mask IP-4 is designed to protect the respiratory organs, eyes, skin of the face and head of a person during emergency, gas rescue and recovery work. These gas masks allow you to work even where there is no oxygen in the air: during accidents, natural disasters, sabotage. The isolating gas mask IP-4 MK is used in an atmosphere unsuitable for breathing, including those containing chlorine (up to 10%), ammonia, hydrogen sulfide. The isolating gas mask IP-4 MR is a regulatory gas mask. The isolating gas mask can be used together with a protective suit. The principle of operation of the isolating gas mask is based on the release of oxygen from chemicals during the absorption of carbon dioxide and moisture exhaled by a person. Isolating gas masks consist of a face part, a regenerative cartridge, a breathing bag and a bag. In addition, the IP-4 isolating gas mask kit includes anti-fog films and, at the customer's request, insulating cuffs can be supplied. The face part of the isolating gas mask protects the respiratory organs from environmental influences, directs exhaled air into the RP and supplies a gas mixture purified from carbon dioxide and enriched with oxygen to the respiratory organs, and also protects the eyes and face. In the isolating gas masks IP-4M, IP-4MK, IP-4 MR the face part is the MIA-1 mask. It has an intercom and an undermask. The regenerative cartridge RP-4 for the isolating gas masks IP-4 IP-4M, IP-4 MK and IP-4 MR provides oxygen for breathing, absorption of carbon dioxide and moisture from exhaled air. The bag is designed for storing and carrying a gas mask. Since the face part of the insulating gas mask does not have sufficient thermal protection properties, it is recommended to work in it with the hood of the protective suit put on the head. The oxygen supply in the RP-4 allows work to be performed in an isolating gas mask under heavy physical loads for 45 minutes, under moderate loads for 70 minutes, and under light loads and in a state of relative rest for 3 hours. It is permissible to work continuously in isolating gas masks with a change of RP-4 for 8 hours. Re-entry into them is permitted only after 12 hours of rest. Periodic use of a gas mask - 3-4 hours daily for 2 weeks. Technical specifications: The weight of the gas mask is no more than 4 kg. The weight of the regenerative cartridge is no more than 2.2 kg. Overall dimensions of the gas mask are 340x163x290 mm. The overall dimensions of the regenerative cartridge are 125x250 mm. The working capacity of the breathing bag is not less than 4.5 l. The duration of the starting briquette is 60-160 sec. The resistance of the excess pressure valve to a constant air flow of 1.5 dm3/min is no more than 392 Pa (40 mm H2O). The closing pressure of the excess pressure valve is not less than 98 Pa (10 mm H2O). The breathing resistance of an isolating gas mask is no more than 1176 Pa (120 mm H2O). The amount of oxygen released by the starting briquette during the decomposition process is not less than 10 l. The temperature of the inhaled gas mixture during heavy physical exertion is no more than 50 degrees Celsius. The time of work in a gas mask is determined by physical exertion and is: in case of heavy physical exertion (running, installation of large parts, carrying heavy objects, clearing rubble) - 30-40 min. with average physical activity (walking, servicing mechanisms, working with indicator devices) - 60-75 min. with light physical activity (technical inspection of equipment, monitoring instrument readings) - 180 min.

Accepted for supply in 2002. Developer: Research Institute of Steel. It is produced by several companies (Armokom, Research Institute of Steel, Kirasa, etc.). The characteristics of helmets produced by different companies may differ slightly. The commercial version of the helmet is designated "P7" (manufacturer - Research Institute of Steel Designed to replace steel helmets SS-68. It is intended for use in the "Barmitsa" combat gear set as a means of armor protection for Ground Forces and Airborne Forces servicemen during all types of combat operations. Provides protection against PM and APS pistol bullets and fragments weighing 1 g at a speed of 560 m/s (1st class of protection of the Ministry of Defense of the Russian Federation). The body material is composite. Protection area – 13 sq.dm. Weight – 1.2 kg. The helmet consists of a body and a sub-body device. The body is made of a composite consisting of ballistic fabric and a special film binder. The under-body device is a belt system that allows the product to be quickly and securely fixed on a serviceman's head, providing protection against impacts with energy of up to 50 J. MODIFICATIONS: 6B7-1. Developer and manufacturer – Armokom. Provides protection for the head from PM and APS pistol bullets, as well as shrapnel (1st protection class). The shell material is organoplastic based on aramid thread with hydrophobic treatment. Anti-fragmentation resistance: fragments weighing 1 g at a speed of 580 m/s and arrow-shaped striking elements weighing 0.87 g at a speed of 215 m/s. Protection area – 13 sq.dm. Weight – 1.2 kg. 6B7-1M. Developer and manufacturer – Armokom. Provides head protection from PM and APS pistol bullets from 5 m, PSM and TT from 50 m, has increased fragmentation resistance (1st protection class). The shell material is discrete fabric materials based on microfilament aramid threads. Protection area – 12.7 sq.dm. Weight – 1.25 kg. The helmet consists of a body and a sub-body device. The body is made of organoplastic based on the latest generation of aramid thread Armos (Rusar) with hydrophobic treatment (6B7-1) or discrete fabric materials based on microfilament aramid threads of the Rusar type (6B7-1M). The under-body device is a belt system that allows the product to be quickly and securely fixed on the soldier's head. The helmet is 2 times lighter than metal and metal-composite analogues The helmet ensures preservation of protective properties when exposed to atmospheric precipitation, 4-fold degassing, disinfection, and decontamination. The level of reduction of the impact impact behind the barrier during shelling and non-penetration exceeds that of standard general-purpose protective helmets The helmet is equipped with summer and winter camouflage covers, a spare under-body device

The PASGT-H protective helmet was adopted in the early eighties. Unlike the old-style protective helmet, which was made of steel, the PASGT-H helmet is made of multilayer reinforced Kevlar 29 fabric and weighs only 1.4 to 1.9 kg. It is available in five sizes and provides protection for the head, forehead, temples, ears and upper neck from shell and grenade fragments, stones and non-ballistic impacts (impacts on surfaces, impacts with various objects such as stones, sticks, beams, etc.). The helmet is not intended to protect against small arms bullets. (From the author. Don't grin ironically, dear reader. No helmet in the world will protect your forehead from a machine gun or rifle bullet. Even an AK-74 bullet can easily penetrate a steel helmet at a range of 800 meters. Even if it were possible to create a bullet-proof helmet, it would not make the fighter's life easier. After all, the force of a blow from even a Makarov pistol bullet is equal to the force of a blow from a 2-kilogram sledgehammer at full swing. Can your spine withstand that?). In all cases, the protective helmet is covered with a camouflage cap made of the same fabric as the uniform itself. If necessary, using an elastic band (clearly visible in the photo), the helmet can be covered with pieces of camouflage net, or improvised camouflage means (branches, grass, pieces of fabric, etc.) can be inserted under the elastic band.

The combat kit of individual equipment (BKIE) "Barmitsa", created in Russia, has no analogues in the world. It is intended for servicemen of motorized rifle units of the Ground Forces, Airborne Forces and special forces operating in combat conditions. The kit takes into account the requirements of maximum unification, compatibility of equipment elements with each other and with combat equipment, and the multifunctionality of their use. "Barmitsa" includes more than 60 both standard and newly developed elements. The basic set of individual equipment "Barmitsa" contains both already known elements of equipment, but of higher quality, and completely new ones; during its development, special attention was paid to the optimal combination of tactical and technical characteristics of all components. Individual means of communication, reconnaissance, navigation and life support, an increased level of protection from enemy weapons and physiological and hygienic characteristics of combat clothing, as well as improved food and medical supplies - all this taken together should lead to a qualitative increase in the combat capability of troops. The BKIE includes new 5.45 mm AN-94 "Abakan" assault rifles and 7.62 mm "Pecheneg" general-purpose machine guns, which are most suitable for motorized rifle units. For each specialty of the Ground Forces, Airborne Forces and special forces units, their own type and purpose of individual and group portable weapons were determined. The use of new personal armor protection equipment by personnel (the "Zabralo" body armor, which can withstand a bullet fired from an AK-74 from a distance of 10 meters, and the "Borit-M" protective helmet), as calculations show, allows for a reduction in losses by more than 20% (if hit by damaging elements of all types, the number of fatalities will decrease by 60-70%). A specially developed portable radio station designed to provide open or closed radiotelephone communication in the "company commander - platoon commander - squad commander - soldier" command link maintains contact with the radio stations of the "Aqueduct" complex, as well as with the radio stations of the old "Arbalet" park. In the cold season, at air temperatures down to -30°C, servicemen will be protected from frost by a new winter suit. Its parts are maximally unified with each other, as well as with similar parts in samples of special and summer field clothing. Feet are protected from low temperatures, dust, sand, dirt, snow by special footwear, the bottom of which is resistant to aggressive environments. Various multifunctional means are used to support servicemen. In addition to its direct purpose, a raincoat-tent can serve as a stretcher, and several tents connected to each other allow you to assemble a large tent. A raid backpack for carrying BKIE elements, including a heat-insulating mat, retains its functional properties even when falling onto rocky ground from a height of ten meters. Combat unloading vests, worn over ammunition and body armor, have a wide variety of attachment points for pouches and pockets depending on the type of weapons carried and the amount of ammunition. They are quickly put on and taken off. And a sleeping bag equipped with a polyethylene mat, mosquito net and inflatable pillow allows for normal rest for 4 hours at temperatures down to minus 20 ° C, as well as for transporting a serviceman weighing up to 120 kg. In case of use of weapons of mass destruction, the kit includes personal protective equipment (PPE) for the respiratory system and eyes, as well as skin, protective gloves and stockings. As part of the R'nD work, autonomous state tests are being conducted to verify the compatibility of standard and developed models of parachute landing equipment as part of the BKIE, and to improve their suspension system. The special elements of the BKIE include survival equipment, a water purification filter, a kit for working in night conditions, and an individual power source.

One of the first to be delivered to the French army in large numbers within the framework of the Scorpion program will be the so-called "future soldier kit" - this is how the "digital" FELIN serviceman kit is often called. In our opinion, the experience gained by French gunsmiths and military personnel during the work on the project will be very useful for domestic creators of weapons, military and special equipment. Especially - special weapons and equipment for personnel of motorized rifle units. The main contractor for the FELIN kit is the French company Sagem Defense Securite. FELIN is an abbreviation for Fantassin a Equipement et Liaisons Integres, which in French means "Integrated equipment and communications for infantrymen." In addition to the traditional FAMAS assault rifle, this kit includes new personal armor protection with improved characteristics, a camouflage jacket and trousers, as well as a wearable personal computer, a radio station, a GPS receiver with the ability to connect a wide range of digital terrain maps, a helmet-mounted information display, optical systems - including sighting systems. Two lithium-ion batteries produced by the Swiss industrial group Leclanche are used as power sources for numerous electronics in FELIN. Work on the program began in the first half of the 1990s, and in 1997–2000, prototypes of the equipment developed within its framework underwent a phase of demonstration tests and initial selection by the military, as well as the approval of final tactical and technical requirements. In early 2000, field tests of the main elements of the kit were conducted: one group of servicemen was equipped with FELIN, while the other used traditional equipment and weapons already available in the French army. Members of the first group emerged victorious from all tests and organized training battles. Even though they were equipped with “old” modifications of the kit, which, compared to the current FELIN, had a significantly greater weight and worsened characteristics of the integrated equipment. Currently, three modifications of the kit have been developed and are being prepared for military testing: for private soldiers, for squad leaders and for platoon leaders. In addition, all equipment and weapons included in the FELIN are divided into three groups according to the type of action and their purpose: individual use, special purpose and collective use. Individual purpose subsystems include six types of equipment: protective clothing with elements of moisture and flame protection and protection against weapons of mass destruction, as well as personal body armor (the basis of this subsystem is a new modification of the unloading vest, onto which personal body armor is hung; the so-called "electronic vest" - a system of optoelectronic and other radio-technical equipment consisting of a personal computer, communications equipment, a "human-machine" interface, a GPS receiver, etc.; a backpack with a container for drinking water; as well as spare magazines for the FAMAS assault rifle and hand grenades); "wearable electronic platform", which is the basis, the "heart" of the FELIN kit and includes, among other things, those optoelectronic and radio-technical means that are part of the "electronic vest" (the platform equipment is built on the basis of a USB 2.0 digital interface and has an open architecture, which allows for the inclusion of new or additional samples of electronic equipment as needed); individual power supply devices; personal weapon - the main weapon of a serviceman using the FELIN kit is the FAMAS assault rifle, but if necessary, 5.56 mm MINIMI light machine guns or 7.62 mm FRS2 sniper rifles with appropriate sights, including infrared ones, can be used. The FAMAS rifle in the FELIN kit is equipped with a small-sized video camera that allows transmitting images to the observation and information collection systems of the squad leader and platoon commander; a combat helmet that includes three components: a protective combat helmet with the ability to use protective transparent face shields, a communications system, and an optoelectronic system. The combat helmet has a built-in protective mask for use in the event that the enemy uses weapons of mass destruction and allows the soldier to take water and food without removing it (the breathing system of the mask uses either an air cylinder or a regenerative filter cylinder). The communications system is integrated into the combat helmet, but can also be used without it. The optoelectronic system includes a helmet-mounted camera based on EBCMOS technology, an information display based on OLED technology (displays graphic, text and video information coming from a personal computer, a video camera and other sources of information, including external ones), and a FELIN communication system that allows for the exchange of information between servicemen and unit commanders (the radio station of any serviceman can be connected to two communication systems simultaneously - at the squad level and at the platoon level, and it is also possible to conduct audio conferences on the battlefield). The radio exchange system has such functions as a soldier's "panic button" that sends a signal to the squad and platoon commander, and a channel for autonomous data transmission, and the platoon commander also has a module for communication with higher command. The communication system is based on the proven and highly effective civilian DECT technology. Special purpose subsystems: military personnel terminal information system; universal IR binoculars (for platoon commander only); case for storing and carrying the FELIN kit components (a standard plastic case type iM3220 manufactured by Hardigg Industries is used). Collective use subsystems: battery charging system from FELIN kits; a family of armoured fighting vehicles designed for use by units whose personnel are equipped with FELIN kits (AFVs of the AMX-10P, VBCI and VAB types). The FELIN equipment will be produced in five versions. It will include weapons, ammunition, body armor, communication and data exchange equipment, a portable computer, a helmet with two displays and a microphone, a GPS receiver, batteries, and a daily ration of food and water. The total weight of the individual equipment will not exceed 25 kilograms. The small arms will be presented in three variants: the FAMAS F1 assault rifle of 5.56 mm caliber, produced by Nexter (formerly Giat), the FR-F2 sniper rifle of 7.62 mm caliber from the same company, as well as the Minimi light machine gun of 5.56 mm caliber, produced by FN Herstal. All weapons will be equipped with new day and night vision sights, as well as improved target designation devices. The presence of video sights will allow firing from cover, without falling into the enemy's field of vision and without endangering life. Ordinary infantrymen will receive sights with Clara electro-optical image intensifiers from Sagem, and unit commanders will receive infrared sights. At the same time, all of them will be able to transmit images in real time within FELIN networks. The equipment set will also include multifunctional binoculars JIM MR from Sagem. They are equipped with an uncooled thermal imaging channel, a safe for eyesight laser rangefinder and a digital magnetic compass. New tactical radios PR4G VS4 from Thales will allow infantrymen to exchange voice and visual information both within units and with higher-level networks. According to the developers, servicemen equipped with FELIN will be able to constantly know the exact location of their comrades in arms, and thanks to the presence of a special "human-machine interface" - will be able to fire from their individual weapons at a target that is not visible to them (for example, without sticking out, fire from around a corner or from cover). Also, regular headphones were replaced with headphones and microphones with vibration capability. Such headphones, for example, are attached to a person slightly below the ear and the soldier perceives information from vibrations that affect the cheekbone and are then transmitted to the person's inner ear. In many ways, as you can see, the French kit is identical in technical equipment to a similar kit intended for equipping US Army servicemen (Land Warrior), which is already being tested in combat in Iraq. Although there are differences: for example, the American kit allows any serviceman in the unit to receive all tactical information – including digital maps indicating the position of friendly and enemy troops – while with FELIN only platoon commanders have the right to access tactical situation maps (squad commanders and private soldiers do not receive them). The initial contract for FELIN was issued to the main contractor in March 2004 - according to its terms, the company must supply the customer with 31,455 such kits to various branches of the French Army: infantry - 22,588, armored troops - 2,801, engineering units - 3,576, artillery - 2,480. The planned date for its introduction into service is 2009, and the first 350 sets must be delivered to the customer by the end of 2008 – in order to equip the first, “test” unit of the French army – an infantry battalion, whose servicemen must conduct a comprehensive test of FELIN. In the event of successful completion of tests between mid-2009 and the end of 2010, the contractor will be obliged to deliver 5,045 sets to equip personnel of five infantry regiments. Although a number of experts have already expressed concerns that there may be a delay – mainly for technical and technological reasons, primarily related to the need to ensure full integration of all subsystems of the kit with the corresponding systems of the ground forces. For example, one of the FELIN advisers stated in an interview with French specialized media that "there are a number of serious problems with ensuring the normal functioning of the helmet-mounted information display and its integration with a number of other systems." According to him, the developer company will not have time to correct the "shortcomings" that have arisen by the end of 2008, which will force the general deadlines for deliveries of the kit to the French ground forces to be shifted. However, the French are not alone in their troubles: the “digital soldier” program implemented in the US Armed Forces also had a hard time. American developers managed to get rid of all technical and technological problems only after two years of painstaking work and numerous military tests.

IdZ (German: Infanterist der Zukunft) is a German set of individual equipment for military personnel, the so-called "future soldier kit". The current version of this program is called IdZ-ES (Expanded System). The IdZ program was launched in 1996, shortly after the creation of a similar program in the United States. The first prototypes were tested during the Yugoslav War, but they showed low efficiency and funding for the program was cut. Development continued after the introduction of Bundeswehr units as part of coalition forces in Afghanistan in 2002. In 2006, the Bundeswehr awarded a $34.4 million contract to Rheinmetall Defence Electronics to develop a prototype of the second-generation IdZ-ES kit and deliver the first 20 units by May 2007 for further testing in Afghanistan.

The Pentagon has been thinking about a computerized and technically equipped soldier since the 80s. But the military department was forced to abandon the Land Warrior project, because the corresponding equipment weighed almost 40 kg, and the batteries that powered the soldier lasted only 4 hours. And so, the Future Force Warrior became the son, first of all, of nanotechnology. It is armed with a machine gun capable of firing not only bullets, but also mini-rockets of 15 mm caliber, equipped with a thermal guidance system so as never to miss the target. The new model can also produce electric discharges to immobilize the enemy. The soldier has glasses. For viewing long distances, up to several kilometers, they are used as binoculars. If you have to look at something close, the glasses begin to act like the Mantis system, copied from insects, allowing you to combine visual, infrared and thermal images into one image, which allows you to answer the main question: "What is behind this door?" Naturally, the soldier can lower a mini-monitor over his eye, allowing him to view the object from different angles. And if the Mantis system is not enough to prevent a threat, electronic sensors signaling explosives or the presence of a person and super microphones capable of hearing a conversation at a distance of 50 m come into play. As for the enemy, the soldier is equipped with equipment that provides information about the temperature, heart rate, and location of the soldier himself. In addition, there is reinforcement made of nanomaterials, which, like an air cushion on a car, is activated when the soldier is hit: it becomes hard, like steel, which bounces off bullets. The same nanomaterials can become nanomuscles, increasing the soldier's strength by 25-30%.

The Russian Defense Ministry is currently developing the "Fighter-21" combat gear for servicemen of the Ground Forces, Airborne Forces and Marines; work on its creation is planned to be completed by 2015 The Russian Defense Ministry is currently developing the "Boets-21" combat gear for servicemen of the Ground Forces, Airborne Forces and Marines. Work on its creation is planned to be completed by 2015, said Associate Professor Vladimir Boyko, a representative of the 3rd Central Research Institute (CRI) of the Russian Defense Ministry, on Tuesday. "The "second generation" kit will include systems for destruction, protection, control, life support and energy supply," Boyko said at a conference within the framework of the international exhibition of security systems "Interpolitex-2009" in Moscow. According to Boyko, the combat gear being created will have no analogues in the world in terms of its effectiveness. At the same time, the existing "first generation" combat gear already surpasses Western models in some parameters, in particular, in terms of protection. According to the expert, the new kit will be 14 kilograms lighter than the previous one, its total weight will decrease from the current 36 kilograms to 22, RIA Novosti reports. "We are currently developing armor material using nanotechnology, new materials will appear in the coming years," Boyko noted. "The kit will include elements of exoskeleton structures (a metal frame that relieves excess stress from human muscles)," he added. The approximate cost of R'nD (research and development work) on the "Boets-21" facility, according to the developers, is about 35 million dollars. At the same time, for example, the United States allocates 1.5 billion dollars for similar work, the speaker said. "The volume of allocations abroad for these programs amounts to billions of dollars and significantly exceeds the volume of allocations allocated for these purposes in our country," noted a representative of the 3rd Central Research Institute of the Ministry of Defense of the Russian Federation.

Despite the name, pseudo-dogs did not descend from dogs, but from wolves. Wild animals that lived in the Zone forests at the time of the disaster became the ancestors of one of the most dangerous mutant species. Pseudo-dogs are as different from mutated dogs as wild wolves are from mongrels. These creatures lead a territorial lifestyle and fiercely attack anyone who invades their territory. Lightning-fast reactions, incredible agility and sharp teeth make the pseudo-dog a very serious opponent, against which even a large mutant or a well-armed stalker will have a hard time resisting. The habitat includes most locations - from the Cordon to the city of Pripyat inclusive. In the depths of the Zone, there are the most seasoned individuals capable of summoning mental relatives.

A large animal, reaching one and a half meters at the withers. In its vitality and aggressiveness, this animal surpasses its relatives outside the Zone and is not inferior to most mutants. Mutagenic processes caused by the effects of radiation and anomalies have significantly affected the appearance of these mammals: in some places the latter have gone bald, and in others, on the contrary, have grown very long, stiff hair. The hooves of the animals have changed their shape and become sharper, acquiring some resemblance to claws; the pupils have become discolored, pigment spots and deep wrinkles have appeared on the bald head. The boars of the Zone tolerate radiation well, which allows them to stay in heavily contaminated areas for a long time. Usually, these animals attack with a running start, trying to rip open the victim's stomach with their fangs or knock it off its feet. It lives in almost all locations adjacent to the borders of the Zone.

The domestic pigs that ended up in the Zone, like many other living organisms, underwent a strong mutation. Since the genes that control metabolic processes were affected, the animal's phenotype changed dramatically. The mutated pig, which stalkers call "flesh", is one of the most obvious examples of the Zone's abuse of nature. These creatures developed a scaly-bony protective cover, the body's ability to regenerate increased significantly, and the nervous system became more complex. Like an ordinary pig, "flesh" is omnivorous, so if hungry, it may well attack a stalker. Its habitat is similar to that of blind dogs and wild boars. Occasionally observed at the stadium in Pripyat.

In fact, these animals are not related to the well-known jerboas, although they are very similar to them. It is not yet possible to indicate the species from which the jerboas of the Zone originated; one can only say that their ancestors were pack animals, most likely rodents. Reaching up to 40 centimeters at the withers, they are much more mobile, aggressive and voracious than the desert animal. They live mainly in dungeons and destroyed buildings, feeding on everything they come across - from plants to gaping stalkers. Sometimes even large animals and mutants become victims of jerboas: attacking in a large pack, they can easily tear apart any creature that has not managed to escape. Quite a rare species in the Zone. Jerboas have been spotted in the "Wild Territory", in the "Dark Valley" and in the city of Pripyat.

These creatures, apparently, were once people, although it is difficult to imagine what conditions could bring a person to such a state. Snorks are crazy creatures, leading a completely animalistic lifestyle, essentially not much different from the predatory monsters of the Zone. They move on all fours, jumping above the ground and constantly sniffing it to catch the scent of their prey. They hunt carefully and prudently, lying in wait for stalkers like predatory animals. Lightning-fast reflexes and hypertrophied muscles allow snorks to make long, precise jumps and tear their prey to shreds in a few seconds. Some individuals have preserved details of army uniforms or individual pieces of equipment, which suggests that they are missing military stalkers. Mostly encountered in the "Wild Territory", "Yantar", "Pripyat" and in underground laboratories.

A semi-legendary monster - seasoned stalkers describe it as a tall, stooped humanoid with many tentacles where its mouth should be. According to them, this creature feeds with its tentacles: by biting into the neck of a living victim, it paralyzes its prey and sucks out its blood; after this procedure, only a dried-up, mummy-like shell remains of the person. The most amazing thing about the bloodsucker is its ability to become invisible. Apparently, these creatures are responsible for the death of a large number of stalkers. Few of the eyewitnesses survived - and, judging by the stories of these lucky ones, bloodsuckers prefer damp places like swamps and dungeons to live. The dungeon of the Research Institute "Agroprom", "Wild Territory", "Arm. warehouses" and "Dark Valley".

A rare mutant found close to the center of the Zone. Outwardly resembles a humanoid with a disproportionately enlarged head. Has developed perception, as well as the ability to control the behavior of less developed living beings. Seasoned individuals are capable of taking control even of people. This is a dangerous opponent, meeting with which even the most experienced stalkers are afraid. Rare species. Was seen in the underground part of the Research Institute "Agroprom", in an abandoned village, not far from the base of the "Freedom" group and in the X-16 laboratory..

Truly supernatural invisible creatures, encountered only in the depths of the Zone; they usually live inside dilapidated buildings. Nothing is reliably known about their origin; there is a legend that these are the spirits of stalkers who were caught in a powerful emission. The mechanism of manifestation of these invisible beings really corresponds to the legends of poltergeists (hence the name) and is distinguished by its diversity: from periodic howling and laughter to dangerous fireballs appearing out of nowhere. Unfortunately, all information about this phenomenon is drawn from vague and rather contradictory stories, the veracity of which is questionable. Lives near underground anomalous sources. Laboratory X-18, rarely in the "Wild Territory"; "Chernobyl Nuclear Power Plant".

A huge carcass consisting of a teardrop-shaped body and a pair of hypertrophied limbs - that's what a pseudogiant is. The arms are used by this creature to move and grab prey. An adult individual can weigh up to two tons and is about two meters tall. The external clumsiness is very deceptive - pseudogiants are swift in their movements, their muscles are simply amazingly powerful, and their bones are as strong as metal. The brain is protected by a thick - about 100 millimeters! - skull; in addition, the spinal cord performs many complex functions. Also amazing is the ability of pseudogiants to form local shock waves on the surface of the earth, affecting all living things within a limited radius.

Only one species of bird survived in the Zone - crows. Other birds could not withstand radiation and did not learn to avoid anomalies. In order not to die in the labyrinth of gravitational disturbances, military pilots use complex detectors, but the secret of the crows will soon drive ornithologists and biologists crazy. There are many theories explaining this phenomenon; however, they all agree that the reason for the miracle is the developed brain of crows, which managed to develop complex reflexes. Crows are immortal, you can not even shoot ...

Cats have not only adapted to life in the Zone, but have even acquired some useful skills. Radiation has hardly changed their appearance. Fast and weak alone, but if you meet a pack of cats, you'd better put a bullet in your forehead. Presumably, their habitat is similar to the habitats of blind dogs, wild boars, and flesh.

Unknown mutations. Either the result of experiments, or the result of the Zone's impact on the remaining people. Hypertrophy of the limb. - Telepathy. - The face is close to human. - Cunning. - Slouches and hides his pseudo-limb under a cloak. From a distance, resembles a hunchbacked tramp. Tries to lure stalkers with conversation or a request. The moment the stalker turns his back, he straightens up and attacks with a hypertrophied limb. If you share food, you can learn interesting information about the Zone. Eats rats, dogs, flesh. With some probability, he can pursue the player. Presumably lives in the areas of the Research Institute "Agroprom" and "Wild Territory". Tries to be closer to places where stalkers gather..

The effects of the Brain Scorcher completely destroy the personality structure, leaving only the corporeal shell. After wandering around the Zone for a while, the mindless bodies begin to turn into real zombies. Only the most primitive reflexes remain, weapons and clothes soon become unusable. As a result, zombies are nothing more than slow half-corpses for whom there are only two effective irritants: food and sleep. Zombies are completely indiscriminate in their choice of food and drink, so their bodies are literally saturated with radiation and toxins. As a rule, these creatures wander aimlessly around the Zone or, like corpses, lie inside abandoned buildings. However, as soon as a zombie senses the close presence of a living person, it immediately tries to attack. Experienced stalkers try to bypass these clumsy, empty shells. Presumably near sources of "scorching" energy..

No one will tell you exactly where this one and a half meter monster came from. No one can even remember where they learned about it. But many stalkers who have ever been on a deep raid remember its image... Advanced stalkers believe that it is a genetic mutant with telepathic abilities, bred by the military. Scientists think that it is the next stage of controller evolution. But neither the former nor the latter dared to go down into the dark basements and dungeons where Burer lives and ask him himself... Presumably lives in underground laboratories, basements and natural caves.

The result of genetic experiments under the "universal animal-defender" (UZhAZ) program. A very smart, strong and fast creature. Hides, goes around from behind. A duplicated system of internal organs and an additional brain makes the process of its killing very problematic. Regeneration. Few biological enemies. In motion resembles a panther. The only worthy opponents in the world of the zone are the Chernobyl dog and the pseudo-giant..

Ugly yellow-white dwarfs live in dungeons, their light-sensitive eyes go blind in bright light. They set traps for stalkers and scientists in the dungeons. They try to block all exits with large debris, rubble and beams. Dwarfs have powerful hands, with which they tear their victims apart. Dwarfs wage war with blind dogs. Their character is extremely vile, nervous anger makes them commit wild and unexpected acts. They have highly developed premonition and telekinetic abilities. Premonition helps to sense the enemy without visual contact and guess his location, and telekinesis allows them to manipulate fairly large objects at a distance, throw them at the enemy, and deliver powerful telekinetic blows to the enemy. Abilities, character, and developed intellect make them one of the most dangerous opponents in the dark places of the Zone. Presumably in underground laboratories, basements and natural caves.

Large and aggressive rat individuals. During hunger they devour their relatives. Usually in a pack there is one rat wolf for every ten rats. Rat wolves make up the backbone of a large pack. They are immune to various poisons and strong radiation. Presumably in underground laboratories, basements and natural caves.

After the disaster, many animals died or left the Zone. The remaining rats multiplied and became the most numerous animals in the Zone. Rats are found in packs, and in rare cases, you can meet them alone. Rats sense dangerous places and carefully avoid them, warning their fellows with a squeak. They are very smart creatures and try to attack the victim from different sides. The victim, surrounded on all sides, has very little chance of survival. Presumably in underground laboratories, basements, and natural caves.

Several generations have changed in the canine family since the first catastrophe, and in each of them the influence of the Zone has become increasingly evident. Mutations and evolutionary adaptation have led to the strengthening of previously weakly expressed canine abilities, often to the detriment of the usual ones. The main physiological changes affected vision - it turned out to be almost useless after the rapid strengthening of the sense of smell: for example, blind puppies survived in the Zone no worse, and sometimes even better, than their sighted brothers. As a result, ordinary dogs soon degenerated here completely, giving way to a new species - blind dogs. These animals perfectly recognize and avoid anomalies, radiation and other invisible dangers that the Zone is teeming with. Like their ancestors, blind dogs hunt in packs: an encounter with a large group of these creatures means serious danger even for a well-armed stalker. They live in almost all locations, excluding only "Pripyat" and "Chernobyl Nuclear Power Plant".

A white ring with blue tints made of light glassy material. When mounted on a thin horizontal rod and slightly spun, it continues to move until it is stopped by extreme force. The first "perpetual motion machine" found in the Zone.

An artifact of organic nature. Consists of deformed tissues of animal origin. When it enters a chemical pollution zone, it begins to "pull" chemicals from the air, transforming them into a thick viscous mass. Radioactive..

A pinkish, translucent granular ingot the size of a palm. When activated by squeezing, it creates a phantom image of the carrier. The image makes movements similar to the carrier's, but moves strictly in a straight line for about a hundred meters, after which it dissipates. The artifact does not work constantly, apparently it needs some time to recharge. Negative properties include a decrease in the carrier's resistance to psi-effects..

A purple stone the size and shape of a mushroom. When it comes into contact with the wearer's body, it neutralizes pain. Very rare, expensive. An indispensable thing for long journeys. Unfortunately, this artifact is found only deep in the Center..

A micaceous formation in the shape of an uneven circle. The color varies from white to light blue, there are also light blue, green and grayish specimens. The bracelet resembles metal in weight and feel, but in fact it consists of the finest scales of an unknown material. It is valued exclusively for its rare beauty. Although it is believed that they have a beneficial effect on health.

A bizarre purple crystal of a round shape. When in contact with the artifact, a person is overcome by apathy, depression, it deprives them of strength. With prolonged contact, a stupor occurs, from which a person may not emerge even after eliminating the cause. Causes irreversible damage to the psyche. Rare artifact, high selling price. It is recommended to wear only in a container. Interestingly, the artifact almost repeats the properties of the mother anomaly..

By its properties, it is a typical healing artifact, has an unusually high price, and, if it is not a panacea, it is capable of curing most diseases known to science, healing even the most severe wounds and bringing a person out of a coma. It is a bluish ball in a greenish frame with a certain concentration of energy, which is gradually consumed when the artifact is used for its intended purpose. After depletion, it turns black. It is known that the artifact is inert in relation to blood, which cannot even stick to the surface of the artifact..

A piece of tightly pressed snowflakes and tiny parts of the anomaly's victims, shaped like a heart. It has a weak psi-effect, looking at the heart, a person plunges into a state of icy calm, concentrates... However, the main property of the artifact is the ability to instantly stop bleeding. Any. A small cut, a bullet wound, lacerations or a limb torn off in the anomaly - you only have to rub the artifact and tie it to the wound, and the blood will stop in a few seconds, the pain will disappear, the affected area will go numb, as after the injection of novocaine. The effect ends in an hour, after the artifact melts..

It is a spiral-shaped substance of milky white color. When activated, it shimmers blue. A very rare artifact with unique healing properties. Most analogues heal only mechanical damage by enhancing the body's regeneration, but this artifact heals any disease. Unfortunately, the artifact only works once, after which it crumbles into fine white sand. Its strangeness lies in the fact that it can heal not the owner, but a person close to him, and at any distance.

A small, fist-sized, bluish-white ball. As if made of ice with small dark blue inclusions. Capable of accelerating any object (bullet) passing through it to unprecedented speeds. Additionally protects against psi effects..

A light gray stone of a round shape, the size of a goose egg. Intricate wavy lines and dots are drawn on it in a darker color. In the Zone, the "Indian stone" can enhance or, conversely, dampen, partially or completely, the properties of other artifacts. On the mainland, it loses its power. It is used in assemblies. The selling price is average..
Quite rare. Found only in the Northern regions of the Zone. The main properties have not yet been studied..

An artifact quite common in the center of the zone. It has an oblong shape of a reddish drop with a green tint. It is formed near the heat anomaly. Increases resistance to high temperatures. Radioactive..
A fairly common emerald viscous substance generated by the aspic anomaly. When wearing this artifact, it creates a special chemical field that protects against harmful effects..

A rainbow sphere the size of an apple, which slowly rotates about one and a half meters above the ground. It emits a thin, barely audible squeak. It has zero gravity, provides weak protection from psi-impact, and heats up strongly in places with increased background radiation. Maternal anomaly: Orchestra (Echo). The selling price is high. Of interest to scientists and collectors. The consequences of an experiment on modified human embryos yielded fruitful results. The resulting mutant samples had truly unique properties. The object received innate resistance to psi-impact and radiation of any intensity. The modified gene structure allowed the creatures to create a specific aura of semi-electric and thermal nature around themselves. Further research in this area will be carried out in three other main areas and is subject to classification.

The most adequate guys in communication without any ideological hang-ups and global goals. They earn their daily bread by searching for artifacts or collecting mutant parts that are needed by domestic and world science. They set up camps, transit points and trading points, which they diligently defend from local wildlife or ill-wishers. Among the latter, there are only criminal elements and the military, with the rest they maintain normal relationships. The only thing that spoils the impression is the predominance of newcomers in the environment. If it is not a sin to talk to promising characters and help with advice, then there are also absolute idiots who have only until the first anomaly or a pack of hungry dogs to live, or even stumble and hit their heads, give up the ghost. Such people can only be helped by kicking them out of the Zone.

Cheerful and cheerful kids without a king in their heads, they consider themselves anarchists. They have a very warm atmosphere at their base. The complete impression is that all they do is loaf around and drink to everyone's health. But this is only in appearance, and in reality they are seriously battle-hardened stalkers. They have no shortage of courage and experience. They are equipped with the smartest bulletproof vests and weapons that can be found. Another thing is that they generally prefer light and long-range weapons, and accordingly, their arsenal increasingly includes sniper rifles or NATO barrels with electronic bells and whistles. They also choose lighter armor that is less heavy at the expense of bullet resistance, but does not restrict movement as much. If they like it better this way, then God help them.
