On September 1, 2004, the entire world was shocked by the news of the seizure of a school building in the North Ossetian city of Beslan by terrorists. On that day, a group of more than 30 terrorists led by Rasul Khachbarov seized the building of Secondary Comprehensive School No. 1. During a festive assembly in the school yard, armed men opened fire over the heads of those gathered and began to drive everyone present into the building. Only a few managed to avoid becoming hostages, taking advantage of the panic. For three days, the terrorists held more than a thousand people in the school building – children, their parents, and school staff. For more than 50 hours, the captives spent in the school building, the terrorists refused to provide them with water, food, and necessary medications. Any attempts to negotiate with the terrorists in a constructive manner and resolve the issue of releasing the hostageswithout forceful intervention were unsuccessful. The only person whom the militants agreed to let into the school building the following day, September 2, was the former President of the Republic of Ingushetia, Ruslan Aushev. The latter managed to convince the captors to let only 25 women and small children go with him. On September 3, a spontaneous operation to free the hostages was carried out. "Tree of Sorrow" in Beslan As a result of the terrorist attack, more than 350 people died - children, local residents, and FSB officers. The remaining hostages (more than 560 people), in addition to the injuries received during the storming of the school building, experienced severe psychological shock. 31 terrorists were killed, one was detained and subsequently sentenced to life imprisonment - Nurpasha Kulayev. On September 17, 2004, Shamil Basayev publicly took responsibility for the terrorist attack in Beslan. The school building was almost completely destroyed after the liberation operation and as a result of the explosion of the explosives that the terrorists had. A year after this tragic event, the "Tree of Sorrow" monument was unveiled at the Beslan memorial cemetery, where the victims of the terrorist attack are buried.

OZM-72 is an anti-personnel jumping fragmentation mine of circular destruction (colloquially - "frog mine"). It was developed in the USSR. It stands for - fragmentation barrier mine. Its origins go back to the German jumping mine SMI-44 from the Second World War. When the detonator is triggered (the standard detonator is MUV 4), the flame ignites the powder retarder, which ignites the powder expelling charge through the central ignition tube. The latter throws the mine's warhead out of the "cup" to a height of about 0.6-0.8 m. The mine is triggered after bouncing by a tension cable, one end of which is secured to the bottom of the mine, and the other to an internal wedge-shaped lock, which activates the fuse that detonates the warhead. This is one of the design differences from its American counterpart, the M-16 mine, which uses a pyrotechnic retarder. If the mine does not fly to the required height, then there is no explosion at all, however, the use of a tension cable increases the reliability of the munition. The damage is inflicted by ready-made striking elements (2400 rollers or balls). In 2010, it was in service in Russia.

The group of nerve agents includes compounds that specifically disrupt the normal functioning of the nervous system, causing convulsions that progress to paralysis. The effect on the nervous system is typical of many potent poisons, but historically this group has only considered derivatives of phosphoric and alkylphosphonic acids of the general formula where R is an alkyl or alkoxy group; R' is an alkoxy, alkyl mercapto, amino group substituted at the nitrogen atom; X is a substituent whose bond with the phosphorus atom is less stable than R and R'. These may be F, CN, acyloxy, dialkylaminoethyl mercapto, nitrophenoxy, or a residue of substituted phosphoric or alkylphosphonic acids. The high toxicity of nerve agents, which manifests itself in any way they enter the body, and the possibility of using all chemical weapons, including missiles, in any climatic conditions have put them in a leading position in the chemical weapons arsenal of foreign armies. The most important among nerve agents are derivatives of methylphosphonic acid GB (sarin), GD (soman) and VX.

This anomaly was one of the first to be discovered back in 2008. It causes impact damage with a gravitational field, like a shock wave from an explosion. It "lives" in one place for an average of a week. During its life, it changes the strength of its impact. The strength of its impact ranges from bruises and contusions to instant death with the launch of the body into orbit. It is detected by distortions in the air above it, swirling foliage, and also by characteristic red-brown spots on the ground in places where losers and idiot newbies "start". It is easily detected by any kind of detectors and by throwing various metal objects into it.

An anomaly of presumably gravitational nature. At the moment of activation, it sucks in everything within a radius of 10-15 meters with terrible force. If you get into the center of the "funnel", there is no chance of survival: the body of both an animal and a person will be compressed into a dense lump, and then torn apart at the moment of the so-called discharge. During the entire period of its existence - on average, this is about a week - the anomaly does not change the place of its manifestation; it can be visually detected during the day by the characteristic movement of air above it, swirling foliage, fragments of dismembered corpses and a characteristic dark spot on the ground in the center. At night, it is extremelydangerous because it can only be detected by detectors or by throwing metal objects.

The name is due to the effect of lifting any living creature into the air with subsequent spinning up to a huge speed. The nature of the "carousel" has not yet been fully explored. It is detected by a light dust whirlwind and scattered fragments of bodies around. It is very important not to miss the moment of the beginning of being drawn into the anomalous whirlwind and not to get into the zone of the strongest effect in the center - only then is there a chance to get away with minimal injuries.
A fireball about a meter in diameter. The heat from it can be felt at a greater distance. It actively moves around closed areas.
What can be described with the words "ball lightning". Of course, a ball, and of course, of an electrical nature... Actively moves along closed trajectories.

At different intervals of time, waves of gravitational emission pass through the Zone, combined with a sharply increased psychotropic effect.during this activity, being in the Zone is mortally dangerous, invisible waves pass through the entire territory, a person without special equipment dies in a matter of seconds, the earth begins to shake, then a series of flashes follows. A brightly glowing sky and a black and white world, the eyes cannot stand the blinding sun. During the emission, you can see phantom formations and haze in the places of gravitational anomalies.
We need to get out of here

It took seven years to launch it into production On May 30, 2013, King Abdullah II bin Al-Hussein of Jordan and CEO of the Rostec State Corporation Sergey Chemezov officially opened a plant in Jordan to assemble RPG-32 Nashshab grenade launchers. The assembly plant belongs to Jadara Equipment 'n Defense Systems, also known as Jordanian-Russian Electronic Systems Co. It took seven years to launch the RPG-32 into production. During this time, the grenade launcher has become a legend and gained wide popularity. This is not surprising, because it is considered the most powerful in the world - it penetrates homogeneous armor up to a meter thick. Therefore, it is very interesting to learn the true history of its creation. To do this, the NVO correspondent met with one of the main creators of the grenade launcher, Vladimir Korenkov, the former general director - general designer of GNPP Bazalt. The Royal Idea At the arms exhibition in Abu Dhabi in March 2005, three groups of interested parties met. The first was the Jordanian group headed by the king, the second was high-ranking representatives of Rosoboronexport and the Federal Service for Military-Technical Cooperation (FSVTS). First, there was a meeting with representatives of the Jordanian generals, who said that they would be interested in taking part in some modern weapons development. King Abdullah II pays great attention to the development of high-tech products. For example, the national Jordanian design bureau King Abdullah II Design and Development Bureau (KADDB) focuses on the development of very high-quality sighting devices from different countries. Moreover, the Jordanians are even authorized users, that is, they have the right to develop or modify something themselves. On the tank theme - this is the king's favorite theme - a tank with an unmanned combat module instead of a turret was created together with South Africa. But there was nothing on the battlefield weapons and ammunition theme. On the same day, all interested parties met in the conference room of the Rosoboronexport pavilion. Within an hour, the king was presented with Russian ammunition products, told about prospects and developments. Vladimir Korenkov says: "Abdallah II liked everything, after some time he expressed interest in ordering the development for Jordan and asked what the Russian side could offer? The idea of a bi-caliber was born immediately. Because it is irrational to carry a heavy grenade launcher when only 10% of the targets on the battlefield require a 105 mm caliber. The rest are easily solved with regular shots of a smaller mass. And then they came up with the name RPG-32. This is a very light grenade launcher, which is also a case for the sighting device. And a system of sealed cartridges with shots of different calibers. Plus a good optical sight, which was later supposed to be developed as a fire control device. But at the same time, another idea was born - to promote these weapons to the markets, primarily to the Middle East countries, using rebranding. Russian weapons, with equal quality, have always been sold at half the price of foreign ones. This could only be changed by rebranding, offering these products from another seller." A fundamentally new grenade launcher was made in just one year. This was facilitated by the serious scientific and technical groundwork that the Bazalt enterprise design bureau had, and a strong team of designers. The "paper" part of the project encountered difficulties - numerous approvals and preparation of documents for the intergovernmental agreement. Bazalt had already had unsuccessful experience of creating similar enterprises in Bulgaria and India. They were literally drowned in bureaucratic procedures at the initial stage. Vladimir Korenkov is sure that without the head of the Federal Service for Military-Technical Cooperation Mikhail Dmitriev and adviser Vladimir Poletov, neither the plant nor the RPG-32 itself would have existed. Opposition to the project was felt from the very beginning, but it was completely unclear where it was coming from. Suffice it to say that the contract came into force after all design work was completed. Bazalt received payment after the RPG-32 was already ready. All experimental design work (RDW) was carried out through the decree of the president and the state intermediary - Rosoboronexport. Its employees were very actively involved in the preparation of all documents on the international part and coordination with departments. Particularly noteworthy is the head of the department of Rosoboronexport Mikhail Bychkov. The financing scheme for the work changed several times - sometimes with own funds, sometimes with a loan. The king was initially ready to pay for everything from his own funds. But in the end, a state loan was received. In parallel, the idea of building a plant in Jordan took shape. Large-module assembly with subsequent possible authorization was supposed there. An internal tender was held to create a sight, and the Belarusian BelOMO offered the best conditions. And in the future, its employees promptly responded to all the needs for changing the characteristics of the sighting complex. They did it very well and for reasonable money. And now they continue to improve the sight, already directly cooperating with Jordan. Educational property Simultaneously with the combat model of the RPG-32, a complex with practical training equipment was made. Previously, training equipment was understood as posters, cut-out models, commander's boxes, etc. At Bazalt, a grenade launcher that fired inert rounds, which were very cheap, fell into this category. The fact is that enterprises received the right to conduct foreign economic activity in the field of service and training simulators, systems and complexes. "Bazalt" used this opportunity to create a new product. As a result, by 2009, the volume of export orders for training shots at the enterprise exceeded orders for combat products. The same with the state defense order. Combat reflexes acquired when using this type of training and combat weapons are absolutely the same. Everything is the same, except for the possibility of barotrauma for the shooter. If a combat shot is an impact of about 160 decibels, in fact, a slight concussion, then a training shot is 135-140 decibels, which is tolerated almost painlessly and allows continuing training shooting. There is no accumulation of hearing injuries and real shooting skills are developed. This property is highly valued by foreign military specialists. We are not talking only about the RPG-32, training equipment was developed for almost the entire line of Russian grenade launchers: RPG-22, RPG-26, RPG-29, RShG-1, etc. A large batch of training equipment was manufactured, including RPG-32, about 10 thousand shots. There were deliveries to Jordan, to domestic special forces for training. But this is where the production of training equipment at Bazalt ceased. And this market is now effectively lost. The reason was the revision of production and design plans by the new management of Bazalt. Vladimir Korenkov was dismissed from the position of general director at the end of April 2009. And he himself did not want to be the general designer, knowing that the enterprise would be restructured and design work would be cut. The state defense order for inert rounds was received by the Planta plant. They used a different technology: they received items from the Defense Ministry arsenal, disassembled them, reloaded them, and for 80% of the price of a new item, they again supplied them to the army. The same ammunition, only without a fuse and explosives. That is, the army lost a combat item and paid for it again in order to receive an inert one. For the same money, Bazalt could supply five times more training equipment without losses at the arsenals. Long-term construction The assembly plant in Jordan was supposed to start operating in the fall of 2009. In April 2009, when Vladimir Korenkov left Bazalt, all activity in this direction ceased. But the Russian-Jordanian agreement remained in effect, and the plant was supposed to be built. In March 2010, King Abdullah II of Jordan, accompanied by Prime Minister of the Hashemite Kingdom Samir al-Rifai and Foreign Minister Nasser Joda, arrived in Moscow to resolve issues of military-technical cooperation. After negotiations with President Dmitry Medvedev and Prime Minister Vladimir Putin, new export agreements were signed in April. Including the establishment of an assembly plant for RPG-32 grenade launchers in Jordan under new conditions. RPG-32 as it is Vladimir Porkhachev either misspoke or didn't know that there is no longer any "Hashim", but there is "Nashshab". By the way, the RPG "Hashim" got its name from the great-grandfather of the Prophet Muhammad, the benefactor of Mecca Hashim ibn Abd ad-Dar, from whom the royal family of the Hashemite Kingdom of Jordan originates. This name was given to the grenade launcher by Abdullah II himself. "Nashshab" can be translated as "shattering", "crusher" or "crusher". Whatever you like best. By the way, it is quite in the spirit of Eastern traditions, where weapons were always given a frightening name. The estimated production capacity of the assembly plant, located 20 km from the capital Amman, is 60 thousand grenade launchers per year. If there are such requests from buyers, of course. For now, 80% of components will be supplied from Russia. In the future, the level of localization is expected to increase. Bazalt claims that the RPG-32 had to be modified, including its appearance. However, no details of the modifications are provided. A comparison of the 2006 and 2013 models, their photographs and videos show that the Nashshab is absolutely identical to the Hashim. The RPG-32 remained in the proving ground version. Instead of camouflage, there are exhibition stars. It still has a technological white cap. And these are all unmasking elements. Judging by this, the grenade launcher has not undergone any modifications. Therefore, its performance characteristics can be cited as unchanged. So, the RPG-32 "Nashshab" is a reusable hand-held multi-caliber multifunctional grenade launcher. Depending on the type of target, it can be equipped with shots of different calibers - 72.5 or 105 mm. The launcher is 36 cm long and weighs 3 kg, including an inward folding optical sight. The range of an accurate shot is 700 m. The launch system is electric, but without a battery or accumulator. The electric impulse is generated by a special generator, which increases the reliability of the grenade launcher. The 72 mm caliber grenade is designed to hit lightly armored targets, shelters, etc., penetrates 500 mm of armor. It weighs 3 kg. A cumulative 105 mm grenade penetrates homogeneous armor about 1000 mm thick and weighs 7 kg. Grenades are contained in special sealed and shock-resistant cartridges. The grenade launcher can withstand up to 200 shots without losing its combat properties. The RPG-32 is very easy to use, any beginner can master it very easily and is guaranteed to hit the target from the second shot. It is enough to catch the target in the sight and shoot. The RPG-32 has not yet been adopted for service with the Russian army. And it will never be adopted. Because a new grenade launcher system is currently being developed in Russia, which will probably be capable of destroying armored vehicles as a class. The tender for its creation for the Russian Ministry of Defense was won by the Bauman Moscow State Technical University by the efforts of the university's structural division - the Special Equipment Research and Production Center. It is headed by Vladimir Korenkov. Here he found support and understanding. The scientific director of the new structural division is Doctor of Technical Sciences, Professor Viktor Selivanov. They are in a hurry to implement breakthrough ideas. They are rushing to arm Russia with the ultimate battlefield weapon. We will find out what they have achieved in a year or two.

TSAMTO, July 18. The EC-645T2 attack helicopter was presented in Astana, Kazinform reports. As the agency notes, Kazakhstan became the fourth country after Germany, France and the USA where the presentation of this machine was held. As reported at the presentation by the commercial director of the company "Eurocopter Kazakhstan Engineering" Karibai Ablezov, "serial production of the EC-645T2 helicopters has not yet begun. The helicopters will be manufactured in Germany and France. We will receive units and components for assembly from these countries. Only final assembly will be carried out in Astana. The first helicopter is planned to be delivered to Kazakhstan in 2014," Kazinform reports. As the agency reminds, Eurocopter Kazakhstan Engineering LLP is a joint venture that was created in 2010 by agreement between the heads of state of Kazakhstan and France. The plant is located near the Astana International Airport. When it reaches its design capacity, it will be able to produce up to 12 helicopters annually. The official opening ceremony of the joint venture with the participation of the President of Kazakhstan Nursultan Nazarbayev took place on June 29, 2012.

This year, the Polish army will receive the POPRAD anti-aircraft missile battery (Poprad is the name of the river). The certification of the system was completed in early June. The military was very interested in this system, and the company (Bumar Electronics SA), which is its developer, will put 2 systems into military testing, and then 4 more systems. The system is a self-propelled anti-aircraft missile system, which is designed to destroy targets flying at low and medium altitudes from 10 meters to 3500 m at a distance of 500 m to 5500 m. It is mounted on the chassis of the Zubr multi-purpose light armored vehicle (manufactured by AMZ Kutno). The air defense system is represented by 4 GROM (Thunder) MANPADS, in the future there will be an upgrade for PIORUN (Lightning) missiles and 4 spare containers. Reloading is done manually. Grom - portable anti-aircraft missile systems of Polish manufacture, designed to combat low-flying air targets. In service since 1995.

Last Wednesday, June 19, Russian President V. Putin, accompanied by Defense Minister S. Shoigu, St. Petersburg Governor G. Poltavchenko and other high-ranking officials, visited the St. Petersburg Obukhov Plant, which is engaged in the implementation of several contracts within the framework of the state defense order. The officials were shown one of the plant's workshops, which housed the machines of the new Vityaz anti-aircraft missile system. After visiting the production facility, the president held a meeting devoted to the current state and prospects of Russian aerospace defense systems. The equipment on display is of particular interest. The Vityaz SAM system was first captured on camera and television, which is why it immediately attracted wide attention from the interested public. The system, developed by the Almaz-Antey concern, will soon replace some modifications of the S-300P family of systems. The Vityaz is designed for air defense of stationary objects and is capable of hitting targets at short and medium ranges. It is noted that the Vityaz uses the same medium-range missiles as the S-400 anti-aircraft system. The Obukhov plant, which is a structural division of the Almaz-Antey concern, produces launchers for the new air defense missile system. All units of the systems are mounted on eight-wheeled chassis manufactured by the Bryansk Automobile Plant. In addition to the vehicle equipped with a launcher with missiles, the anti-aircraft system includes a command post and an all-aspect radar station. The characteristics of the Vityaz have not yet been announced, but representatives of the developer are talking about the upcoming replacement of early models of the S-300 systems. This allows us to roughly imagine the capabilities of the new air defense missile system. After the demonstration deployment of the complex systems, which took place right in the workshop, the president was invited into the cabin of one of the vehicles. There, V. Putin examined the instrumentation and asked several questions to the engineers who developed the project. During the demonstration of the Vityaz complex, the general director of the Almaz-Antey concern V. Menshchikov spoke about the future of the new development. According to him, an agreement has already been reached with the Ministry of Defense and missile testing will begin this year. Thus, the new air defense system will be sent to the troops in the coming years. At the meeting that followed the small "exhibition", V. Putin emphasized the importance of the current work. He recalled that the opinion about the so-called first disarming strike is increasingly being expressed. According to the president, the military and the defense industry should take into account the possibility of such events when developing the armed forces. About 3.4 trillion rubles are planned to be allocated for the development of aerospace defense, which is one of the highest priority areas, by 2020. This will improve the capabilities of the troops protecting the country's airspace, as well as prepare to repel possible threats. It was noted at the meeting that the development of VKO, rather than "classical" air defense, is in some ways a forced measure. The fact is that neighboring states are actively engaged in the topic of medium-range ballistic missiles, and Russia needs to take this into account. Our country at one time refused to create and operate such systems, but for some of our neighbors, medium-range missiles are of great interest. For this reason, defenses are needed that are capable of countering such threats. Work on the creation and construction of new air defense and air defense systems is already underway, and some of the results of these programs will be shown to the public in just a couple of months. According to Putin, at the upcoming international aerospace show MAKS-2013 (Zhukovsky), the Russian defense industry will demonstrate the latest models of some air and space defense systems. At the same time, the president did not specify the specific type of equipment planned for display. Speaking about the serial production of new equipment, Putin noted that the main obstacles to the construction of anti-aircraft systems are bureaucracy and red tape. However, despite these problems, production and design work must proceed according to schedule. The relevant managers must bear personal responsibility for missed deadlines, delays and similar phenomena. It is noteworthy that a bill has already been submitted to the State Duma that provides for personal financial penalties for negligent officials and businessmen. In accordance with this bill, responsible persons who disrupt the implementation of the State Defense Order will pay fines, and various restrictions will be imposed on organizations. During the meeting, the President recalled the main objectives pursued by the current state rearmament program. By 2015, the Aerospace Defense Forces must renew their equipment by half, and by 70% by 2020. The implementation of such plans requires the renewal of production facilities and the improvement of their work. Certain measures are already being taken for this, and the Obukhov Plant is no exception. In order to improve the efficiency of the Obukhov Plant and a number of related enterprises, a North-West regional center is currently being created as part of the Almaz-Antey Air Defense Concern, which will include five St. Petersburg plants. As V. Putin said, such a measure will optimize production costs and transportation processes. It is the Obukhov Plant that will play a leading role in this unification. In addition, the enterprise is currently undergoing reconstruction, which should result in the renewal of production capacities and, as a consequence, an increase in capabilities. The first results of all this work are already visible. Thus, in the current 2013, the Obukhov Plant was able to conclude contracts in the total amount of 12 billion rubles within the framework of the State Defense Order. Last year, this figure was four times less. Among the new types of equipment, the production of which will begin at the updated enterprises in the near future, will be the new anti-aircraft missile system "Vityaz". The estimated date for its adoption into service is 2016. The exact dates will become clear later, when the testing of individual elements and the entire system as a whole is completed.

Modern nuclear weapons are currently in stock in many countries of the world. Naturally, all types of nuclear weapons can be subdivided. For example, nuclear weapons (taken from http://nuclear-weapon.ru/) include: artillery, which uses nuclear warheads; submarines; nuclear mines. Each of the above-mentioned supplies, in turn, is distinguished by power. They are conventionally divided into: ultra-small, small, medium, large, and also super-large. The type of any ammunition depends entirely on its purpose and design. On the body of each ammunition of this nature there are special recesses, with the help of which it is possible to check the serviceability of the automatic system of the nuclear charging device. Training charges are specially marked, and in some countries the containers in which nuclear weapons are transported are marked with paint of a certain color. In Pakistan, this type of weapon is also developed. When many countries began developing nuclear weapons, Pakistan also developed a special nuclear program. This happened in the seventies. In essence, this program was aimed at creating nuclear weapons using uranium. According to the available data, this state has special technological capabilities of a fairly high class in order to quickly produce nuclear devices of a certain weight and power. A fairly objective condition for confirming this aspect is the fact that Pakistan is independent in providing special fissionable substances, because in many areas of this state there are large reserves of uranium ores necessary for these purposes. In addition, many sources report that Pakistani scientists have begun to show direct interest in using plutonium for the purpose of creating nuclear warheads. The important thing is that for the successful implementation of the above-mentioned program, this country has all the most important conditions for this. The state has the necessary scientific personnel, modern technology, which is used for the purpose of enriching uranium as much as possible. In addition, the plant in a large city not only supplies nuclear fuel to the stations, but also creates new reserves - directly for future stations. In fact, the nuclear program here, under the influence of such important factors, is successfully developing further..

MOSCOW, July 16 — RIA Novosti. On Tuesday, MiG-29K (KUB) aircraft made their first training flights simulating landing approaches to the aircraft deck of the Russian NITKA simulator under construction (Yeisk, Krasnodar Krai), a representative of the Southern Military District (SMD) told RIA Novosti. This is how he commented on reports in some media outlets that the Su-25UTG aircraft had completed its first flight from a naval aviation test simulator. "Today there were two test flights of the MiG-29KUB, during which the aircraft performed a simulated landing on the deck of an aircraft carrier. The flights will last for two weeks. About ten flights will be made," the agency's source said. He emphasized that the main purpose of the flights is to check the lighting and technical equipment of the simulator deck. In particular, the Saturn, Luna-37, and MTK-201 complexes will be assessed. The test flights are being conducted by pilots of the RSK MIG; the Southern Military District only provided airspace for the tests, the district representative noted. Earlier, the Commander-in-Chief of the Russian Navy, Admiral Viktor Chirkov, told RIA Novosti that the Russian simulator in Yeysk would begin operating at the turn of 2013-2014. Director of Spetsstroy Grigory Naginsky told the agency that planes would begin flying on the Russian simulator in September-August 2013. The NITKA aviation complex (ground test training complex aviation) is used to train pilots of deck aviation. This simulator is an airfield with a steel airfield in the form of a ship's deck, equipped with a ski jump and arresting gear. The dimensions of the airfield are equivalent to the dimensions of the flight deck of the heavy aircraft carrier "Admiral of the Fleet of the Soviet Union Kuznetsov". The only NITKA complex created in the USSR is located at the Novofedorovka airfield (near the city of Saki in Crimea). After the collapse of the USSR, the complex passed to Ukraine..

The latest fifth-generation aircraft is equipped with a unique pilot breathing system The Russian fifth-generation fighter Sukhoi T-50 is equipped with an unlimited oxygen supply system in the pilot's mask. It is needed for flights at an altitude of over 4 km - without oxygen, the pilot begins to "fall asleep" and loses consciousness. In all existing Russian combat aircraft, oxygen is supplied from cylinders, which sooner or later run out. In the T-50, it is produced from the air by a special system developed by the Zvezda research and production enterprise. — Oxygen is produced here from the air, the system takes it from the engine compressor and supplies it to the pilot's breathing mask. Flight time no longer depends on the oxygen supply, and the pilot can fly with several refuelings, unlike existing aircraft, where the pilot constantly needs to check whether he has run out of oxygen before the fuel, — Mikhail Dudnik, chief specialist at NPP Zvezda, told Izvestia. According to him, in addition to an unlimited amount of oxygen, the system allows saving the payload of the combat vehicle - the entire oxygen production unit weighs about 30 kg, while oxygen cylinders for two hours of flight weigh 90 kg. “This is especially important for fighter aircraft, where there is always a shortage of space and weight and where it is impossible to store oxygen in large quantities, as on large bombers,” Dudnik explained. According to him, the system has been developed over the past five years and is now installed on four experimental T-50 aircraft, which are undergoing testing in the Moscow region. “The first experimental T-50s, which are manufactured in Komsomolsk-on-Amur, flew under their own power, and they had to, of course, refuel, but there was no need to replenish oxygen supplies,” Dudnik explained. No modern aircraft can do without oxygen - at altitudes above 4 km there is nothing to breathe. And if for a passenger or transport aircraft the presence of bulky cylinders on board is practically unnoticeable, then for a fighter, where every centimeter and gram is calculated, a weight reduction of 60 kg is very significant. A similar installation for the Russian Air Force Yak-130 combat training aircraft was created on the basis of the BKDU for the T-50 fighter, and this year the deck fighters MiG-29K will also receive an oxygen system. Having assessed the advantages of such systems, the Algerian and Malaysian armies have already ordered them for their Su-30 fighters. — The first experimental Yak-130s had a standard air installation with cylinders, — recalls test pilot Roman Taskaev. — In 1999, we ferried such a Yak-130 to Italy to the Aermacchi plant. We took oxygen so that it would last for the entire flight, but due to partial depressurization of the cylinders, its consumption increased, and we had to land at the airfield in Bratislava for refueling. With the new system, such problems cannot occur in principle. In the Russian army, oxygen is traditionally "mined" on the ground. This process requires trained personnel and complex special equipment. If the technology is violated, there is a high probability of an explosion. — Currently, the airfield technical support battalions include oxygen-producing automobile stations, — former Air Force Commander-in-Chief Anatoly Kornukov told Izvestia. — To service them, soldiers and sergeants who have undergone six months of training in this specialty are needed, and officers generally study for five years. Due to the high fire hazard of oxygen, the selection for these specialties is very strict. According to Kornukov, the new oxygen system will help reduce the number of airfield employees and increase the efficiency of using fighters at long distances. There will also be no problems at field airfields where there is no oxygen-producing equipment. The onboard oxygen-producing station system, designated OBOGS, is also in use in the US Army - it is installed on fifth-generation F-22 aircraft and F-18 carrier-based fighters.

TSAMTO, July 17. At the Tsabal mountain training ground in Abkhazia, air defense units of the Russian military base conducted training to repel air attacks by an enemy, the press service of the Southern Military District reported. In total, about 100 military personnel took part in the training, and several dozen units of weapons, military and special equipment were also used. In mountainous and wooded terrain, anti-aircraft missile units worked on issues of capturing, tracking, and simulated destruction of single and group air targets of a simulated enemy. Particular attention was paid to the practical development of the newest mobile small-sized three-coordinate radar station (RLS) "Garmon", which was delivered to the unit at the end of last year. The new radar provides detection and tracking of various air objects, determination of their nationality, and automatic transmission of route information to automated control systems. The station’s small size and weight ensure its prompt delivery to destinations, including in hard-to-reach and mountainous areas. During the training, the issues of occupying launch positions on the move, camouflaging them, organizing the communication and control system, bringing the equipment from the marching position to the combat position were worked out. The personnel also completed a number of introductory tasks on reconnaissance, detection, tracking and destruction of the simulated enemy's targets. In the near future, tactical exercises with live fire will be held with air defense units of the military base at a training ground in the Krasnodar Territory; at the end of the summer, air defense specialists of the unit will take part in a tactical-special exercise at the Kapustin Yar training ground (Astrakhan Region), according to a statement from the press service of the Southern Military District.

MOSCOW, July 11 — RIA Novosti. The latest combat gear for Ground Forces servicemen, "Ratnik," will be presented to the general public for the first time at the Russia Arms Expo-2013 exhibition, Deputy Prime Minister Dmitry Rogozin reported on his Twitter microblog on Thursday. "The Ratnik has been accepted into service. The combat gear has really turned out to be decent. We will show it at RAE (Russia Arms Expo-2013, to be held at the end of September) in Nizhny Tagil," Rogozin wrote. The ground forces have not yet confirmed the information about the adoption of the Ratnik into service. During a visit to the Degtyarev Plant in Kovrov on June 4, Rogozin said that the Ratnik could be accepted into service within a month. State tests of the Ratnik began in April 2013, and preliminary tests began in September 2012 and took place at the 27th Separate Motorized Rifle Brigade (Moscow Region). "Ratnik" is a Russian combat gear for the "soldier of the future", which combines modern small arms, effective protection kits, reconnaissance and communications equipment, a total of about 10 different subsystems. In addition to its combat functionality, it is designed to provide effective protection for the soldier from various damaging factors on the battlefield. The "Ratnik" kit includes about 40 different elements, including small arms, aiming systems, protection, electronic communications, navigation and target designation. Russian Arms Expo is an international exhibition of weapons, military equipment and ammunition, which has been held since 1999 every two years at the Staratel proving ground of the Nizhny Tagil Institute for Metal Testing. It alternates with the international exhibition of protective equipment Russian Defence Expo, which is also held at the Staratel proving ground every two years. This year, Russia Arms Expo will be held from September 25 to 28 under the patronage of the Government of the Russian Federation. The organizers of the exhibition are the Ministry of Industry and Trade of Russia and the Government of the Sverdlovsk Region, and Dmitry Rogozin is in charge of preparations for the event.

Peru has seen the laying of a new Makassar-class landing and helicopter dock ship, which is being built under license from the South Korean company Dae Sun Shipbuilding 'n Engineering. As reported by Jane's, the keel-laying ceremony for the ship took place at the SIMA shipyard in Callao on July 12, 2013. The keel-laying of a second Makassar-class ship is also expected to take place soon. The contract for the licensed construction of the landing ships was signed in 2012. According to the terms of the agreement, the Peruvian Navy should receive both ordered landing ships in 2015. The ships will have a displacement of 7.3 thousand tons. They will be able to transport up to 450 marines each, and their air group will include two multi-purpose helicopters and landing craft. The ships will also be able to transport up to 14 amphibious armored combat vehicles. The Makassar class ships are currently only used by the Indonesian Navy, for which they were originally designed and built by a South Korean company. The Indonesian Navy currently operates four of these ships: the Makassar, Surabaya, Banjarmasin and Banda Aceh. The Indonesian ships are capable of speeds of up to 16 knots and have a range of ten thousand miles.

On the eve of Navy Day, on July 26, a ceremonial laying of the new-generation nuclear submarine cruiser Novosibirsk will take place at the Sevmash defense shipyard in Severodvinsk. As the ITAR-TASS correspondent was informed today by the plant's press service, this missile carrier will be the third in the line of multi-purpose nuclear submarines /SPS/ of the Yasen project, developed by the St. Petersburg Marine Engineering Bureau Malakhit. "The project uses many technical solutions that have not previously been used in domestic submarine shipbuilding," the shipyard's press service said earlier. "The ships of this series will become the embodiment and concentration of unique, cutting-edge Russian developments in the field of military weapons, weapons, technical and electronic systems, and main power plants." The lead submarine of the series, Severodvinsk, project 885 /code name "Yasen"/, is currently undergoing state trials at sea. Work is underway on the submarine Kazan, the keel-laying ceremony of which took place in 2009, in the main slipway shop of Sevmash. Kazan and Novosibirsk are being built according to a modernized project - 885M. The changes will affect the element base of the electronic weapons systems, modernized equipment and materials, which will be supplied exclusively by the Russian manufacturer. According to the state armament program, Sevmash must build seven nuclear submarines of the Yasen and Yasen-M classes by 2020. According to the Russian Naval Doctrine, in the future, submarines of this project, which are being built in large series, will become the main multi-purpose nuclear submarines of Russia.

TSAMTO, July 18. The Vietnamese Air Force is considering the possibility of acquiring Belarusian-made RV-01 Vostok-E mobile solid-state digital two-coordinate meter-range radars. These radars can effectively detect modern stealth fighters, including the J-20 being developed in China. The station is characterized by increased jamming immunity, stealth, reliability and mobility. Several Vietnamese and Taiwanese resources, citing Russian sources, reported that the station, developed by the Belarusian JSC Design Bureau Radar (JSC KB Radar), is intended to replace the outdated Soviet-era P-18 radars in the Vietnamese Air Force. According to the developers, the Vostok-E radar is capable of detecting stealth fighters, including the American F-117A, in conditions of interference at a range of 74 km, and in their absence – up to 350 km. Sources say Belarus plans to sell the Vietnamese Armed Forces about 20 Vostok-E radars and send advisers to train personnel in the use of the new equipment. A group of Vietnamese specialists have already undergone training in Belarus in June this year. According to experts, strengthening military-technical cooperation with Belarus will allow Vietnam to diversify imports by countries-suppliers of military equipment.

The design bureau of the Nizhegorodsky Teplokhod plant has developed a project for the 02320 Kazak landing craft, which can be placed in the dock chamber of the Mistral-type landing helicopter-carrying dock ship. As reported by the Central Naval Portal, the project for the new craft was presented at the International Maritime Defense Show in St. Petersburg. The length of the landing craft is about 28 meters, and the width is about seven meters. The boat can reach speeds of up to 11 knots and carry two armored personnel carriers of the BTR-80 type or up to one hundred landing personnel. The "Cossack" is equipped with three remotely controlled modules: one with a 12.7-millimeter machine gun, and the other two with automatic 30-millimeter grenade launchers. According to the project, the dock chamber of the Mistral helicopter carrier (two ships are currently under construction for the Russian Navy; a contract for two more such landing craft may be signed) can accommodate four Project 02320 boats. The new boat can be used not only to deliver troops, but also to deliver emergency rescue equipment and to conduct diving operations at depths of up to 60 meters. The future of Project 02320 has not yet been determined. Nizhegorodskiy Teplokhod intends to offer the new boat to the Russian Navy command; in the fleet, the Kazak may replace or supplement the Project 1176 Akula and Project 1785 boats already in service.

Irkut Corporation Develops Su-30SM Program At the State Flight Test Center named after V.P. Chkalov of the Ministry of Defense of the Russian Federation (city of Akhtubinsk, Astrakhan Region), a preliminary conclusion was signed with JSC Irkut Corporation (part of the United Aircraft Corporation) on special joint flight tests of the multifunctional two-seat fighter Su-30SM. Unique opportunities "The Su-30SM aircraft will significantly enhance the combat capabilities of the Russian Air Force," said Chief of Aviation - Deputy Commander-in-Chief of the Air Force for Aviation, Hero of Russia Colonel Sergei Kobylash. "The Su-30SM's ability to simultaneously detect and destroy several targets and its super-maneuverability are unique. Modern fighters will be delivered to the Air Force not in single units, but in entire squadrons," he noted. In turn, the President of Irkut Corporation, Oleg Demchenko, emphasized that Irkut Corporation, working in close cooperation with Sukhoi, will make every effort to timely fulfill the state contract for the supply of Su-30SM fighters. The multi-role super-maneuverable fighter Su-30SM is a further development of the family of combat aircraft of the Su-30MK type. Specialists of JSC "Company" Sukhoi "adapted the fighter to the requirements of the Russian Air Force in terms of radar systems, radio communications and state identification, ejection seat and a number of support systems. Changes were also made to the composition of the armament. The first contract for the supply of multi-role fighters Su-30SM to the troops between the Ministry of Defense of the Russian Federation and JSC "Corporation" Irkut "was signed in the spring of last year. First flight "Industrial Weekly" has already written that one of the most significant achievements in the life of Russian mechanical engineering last year was the success of the Su-30SM program. It is noteworthy that the first flight of this unique fighter took place at the airfield of the Irkutsk Aviation Plant - a branch of JSC "Irkut Corporation" just before the celebration of the traditional Machine Builder's Day in the country. As reported at the time by the joint official press release of the Irkut Corporation and the Sukhoi Design Bureau, the Su-30SM aircraft was piloted in its first flight by test pilots of the Sukhoi Design Bureau: the crew commander was test pilot 1st class Sergey Kostin, the navigator was honored test navigator of the Russian Federation Pavel Malovechko. The flight lasted two hours and went without any comments. The multi-role super-maneuverable fighter Su-30SM is a further development of the family of combat aircraft of the Su-30MK type. Specialists of JSC "OKB Sukhoi" adapted the fighter to the requirements of the Russian Air Force in terms of radar systems, radio communications and state identification, ejection seat and a number of support systems. Changes were also made to the composition of the armament. Promising contract As already noted, the contract for the delivery of new fighters to the troops was signed in the spring of last year, or more precisely, in March 2012. The contract provides for the delivery of 30 Su-30SM multi-role fighters by 2015. As numerous media outlets have written, this contract between the Russian Ministry of Defense and Irkut Corporation OJSC has become a significant event both in the life of the domestic aviation industry and in terms of strengthening the defense capability of the Russian Air Force. As stated by the high representative of the Russian Defense Ministry when signing this document, the entry into the Armed Forces of modern super-maneuverable two-seater Su-30SM fighters will significantly increase the combat power of the Russian Air Force. In addition, the technical capabilities of the aircraft will allow achieving a higher level of pilot training, which is especially important in connection with the increase in the volume of purchases of new-generation combat aircraft. The fact that the Russian Air Force intends to purchase a batch of new Su aircraft was first mentioned during the last MAKS-2011 air show. Indeed, the state armament program for 2011-2020 provides for the delivery of Su-30SM fighters to the Air Force and the Navy. Presumably, the Su-30SM will be based at the Gvardeysky airfield in Crimea, where they are to completely replace the Su-24 bombers currently stationed there. However, the contract signed in March turned out to be even larger than planned - 30 aircraft instead of 28, and the talk was only about equipping the Air Force. Transfer of aircraft And in the autumn of last year, the official transfer of two Su-30SM to the Russian Air Force pilots took place. Oleg Demchenko, President of Irkut Corporation, commented then: "We have been working for export for many years, and now we have begun to supply aircraft to the Motherland. Nine of our Yak-130s are already flying at the Borisoglebsk training center, and today we are transferring the first two Su-30SM fighters to the Russian Air Force. This is a historic event for our team, for the Sukhoi company, for the entire United Aircraft Corporation." The head of the Military Training and Scientific Center of the Russian Air Force, the Air Force Academy named after Professor N. Ye. Zhukovsky and Yu. A. Gagarin, Aleksandr Kharchevsky, who was present at the transfer of the equipment, said: "The Su-30SM allows us to increase the combat capabilities of the Russian Air Force." The famous military pilot, who headed the Center for Combat Use and Retraining of Flight Personnel of the Air Force named after V. P. Chkalov for many years and has experience piloting fighters of this type, noted the capabilities of the Su-30SM to simultaneously detect and destroy several targets and its super-maneuverability. Technical details The multi-role super-maneuverable fighter Su-30SM is a further development of the family of combat aircraft of the Su-30MK type. This family is certainly one of the most famous in the world fighter aviation. A little history… The Su-30MK multi-role combat aircraft, manufactured by the Irkut Corporation at the Irkutsk Aviation Plant, is one of the most advanced and sought-after fighters in the world. The Su-30MK from the Irkut Corporation became the world's first export fighter equipped with an onboard radar with a phased antenna array (PAR), as well as the world's first serial combat aircraft with super-maneuverability. The Su-30MK has been in serial production at the Irkutsk Aviation Plant since 2002. The Su-30MK program has no equal in the entire history of military-technical cooperation between the Russian Federation and foreign countries in a number of parameters. Export volumes of this fighter are estimated in hundreds (in units) and billions (in dollars). The Su-30MK is designed to achieve air superiority, intercept air targets, patrol and escort, and destroy ground and surface targets (suppress enemy air defenses, block enemy air actions, and provide close air support). The aircraft can perform tasks to counter electronic warfare systems and conduct early warning, as well as provide operational control of a group of aircraft performing a joint mission. Due to the duplication of flight controls, the Su-30MK can be used for flight and combat training. In technical terms, the Su-30SM is a development of a famous program for the Russian Air Force. It is a two-seater aircraft with thrust vectoring engines, which provides it with super-maneuverability. It is equipped with a phased array radar, can carry a wide range of missile and bomb weapons, and can hit all types of air, land, and sea targets. The length of the aircraft is 21.9 m, the height is 6.36 m; the maximum takeoff weight is 34,500 kg; the maximum speed is 2,125 km/h; the combat radius is 1,500 km. The combat load is 8,000 kg. Unlike its predecessors in its family, the Su-30SM avionics are built on the principle of open architecture, which simplifies the integration of new systems and weapons into the avionics. A quote from one expert: "The Su-30SM, with its unique combat qualities today, allows for the creation of compact air groups equally well suited to combat air, land and sea enemies." JSC Scientific and Production Corporation Irkut (part of the United Aircraft Corporation) occupies a leading position among Russian aircraft manufacturing enterprises and is a vertically integrated holding whose activities are aimed at the design, production, sale and after-sales service of military and civil aviation equipment. The corporation united leading domestic manufacturers and developers in the field of aircraft construction - Irkutsk Aviation Plant, Taganrog Aviation Scientific and Technical Complex named after G. M. Beriev, JSC "OKB named after A. S. Yakovlev", CJSC "BETA IR" and others. In March 2004, for the first time in the history of the Russian aircraft and defense industry, the Corporation successfully conducted an initial public offering (IPO) of 23.3% of its shares on the stock exchange among Russian and foreign private and institutional investors. Since November 2006, the company has become part of the Russian United Aircraft Corporation (UAC). The enterprises of the Irkut Corporation employ over 14 thousand people, who develop and manufacture a wide range of high-tech products. Currently, the order portfolio is over $6 billion. The company's revenue has doubled over the past five years. The Corporation accounts for over 15% of the Russian arms export market. The main product of Irkut Corporation is the Su-30 family of combat aircraft. The company is the prime contractor for the Su-30MKI production program for the Indian Air Force. As part of the diversification of its product range, the Corporation also develops and manufactures Yak-130 combat training aircraft, unmanned aerial vehicles, and components for Airbus family passenger airliners. Recently, the Corporation has been actively working on creating a new MS-21 passenger aircraft. Over the past seven years, the Corporation has been included in the rating of 100 world leaders in the military-industrial complex according to the authoritative American publication Defense News. Based on the results of 2007-2010, Irkut was recognized as the "Company of the Year" in the nomination "Industry. Military-industrial complex". In 2012, for the fourth time in a row, the Ministry of Industry and Trade of Russia recognized Irkut Corporation as the winner of the competition for the title of "Best Russian Exporter of the Year" in the nomination "Aircraft Manufacturing (Aircraft Manufacturing)". Irkutsk Aviation Plant (branch of Irkut Corporation) became the first enterprise in Russia to receive certificates of compliance with Airbus and EN9100 standards.

TSAMTO, July 10. In the near future, Paraguay plans to sign a contract for the purchase of three new radars, each of which is estimated to cost $14 million, said the country's Defense Minister Maria Liz Garcia de Arnold. The delivery is expected to be completed within 24 months from the date of signing the contract, and payment will be made over 10 years, Defensa.com reports. Paraguay's Ministry of Defense has been lobbying the country's parliament for the purchase of new radars since November 2012. The stations are planned to be located in Asuncion, Mariscal Estigarribia and Ciudad del Este. According to national media, the Paraguayan Air Force has selected the EL/M-2082 L-band solid-state phased array radars from the Israeli company Elta Systems. The radars, with a maximum target detection range of 480 km, can be used as part of the air defense and air traffic control system. The Paraguayan Air Force previously purchased two EL/M-2106NG radars from Elta Systems. The purchase is being carried out as part of the Paraguayan Air Force modernization program, which includes the acquisition of a new generation of radars. The second phase of modernization is scheduled to begin in 2014. It will include the acquisition of a new light combat aircraft. Among the platforms being considered by the Paraguayan side are the Chinese K-8 Karakorum, the Russian Yak-130, the Czech L-159 ALCA, the Argentine AT-63 Pampa-2 and the Brazilian EMB-314 Super Tucano.

Azerbaijan is the second country after Turkey that widely uses the JNG-12 "Bora" sniper rifle. This was reported to APA by the Turkish MKEK. According to MKEK specialists, in 2009 this sniper rifle was transferred to the Border Troops of Azerbaijan. In subsequent years, this weapon was also exported to the Ministry of Defense and the Internal Troops of Azerbaijan. The rifle has proven itself in the practice of Azerbaijani servicemen and has given high results. The company has reviewed proposals from Azerbaijani military personnel regarding the improvement of the sniper rifle. The Turkish side also considers it possible to jointly produce this rifle with Azerbaijan. It should be noted that the JNG-12 "Bora" sniper rifle was created and produced by MKEK by order of the Turkish Gendarmerie Forces, fires 7.62x51 mm. caliber cartridges. The rifle buttstock is adjustable depending on the shooter's wishes..

A new modification of the flamethrower, ordered by the Ministry of Defense, will allow special forces to more effectively fight the enemy in urban areas The Ministry of Defense has decided to update the Shmel infantry flamethrowers, replacing them with a new modification, the Shmel-M. The first batch of 200 flamethrowers will be purchased for the Senezh special forces unit by the end of the year. As the Tula Instrument Design Bureau, which developed the new flamethrower, told Izvestia, the main difference between the Shmel-M and its predecessor is a new incendiary mixture and an improved design of the thermobaric munition. "The mixture has been improved by adding special substances that prevent the aerosol cloud from spreading in open areas, and the projectile itself has been improved. At the same time, the appearance has remained the same," explained one of the developers. Modern flamethrowers fire thermobaric ammunition with a special incendiary mixture. When it hits the target, the mixture is sprayed, forming a cloud of explosives. The explosion of such a cloud creates a fireball and a low-pressure zone at the site of the explosion (it “sucks” all the oxygen out of the air), and people die and buildings collapse from the pressure drop and high temperature. "Shmel" remains the only Russian portable flamethrower. In addition to it, the Russian army has in service the heavy flamethrower systems (TOS) "Buratino" and "Solntsepek". A representative of the military-industrial complex, who is participating in the work to create the new flamethrower, told Izvestia that the Shmel-M is almost equal in explosive force to a 152-mm high-explosive shell fired by howitzers. — The new flamethrowers are 3-4 times more powerful than the old Shmels in terms of shock wave force, and thanks to this, in urban combat, special forces will be able to destroy not only small buildings, but also 2-3-story brick, panel, and even stone buildings. The Ministry of Defense is currently purchasing new models of thermobaric flamethrowers PDM-A "Shmel-M" only for the Special Purpose Center "Senezh", which is stationed in Solnechnogorsk near Moscow. As the center's management told Izvestia, the new flamethrowers are designed for combat operations in urban conditions. — The special forces solve complex problems, so the weapons and equipment must meet these requirements. They always have special equipment. For example, since the special forces work at night using night vision devices (NVD), the standard optical daytime sight PDM-A is not suitable. Therefore, in the special forces version, the sight is made so that, by combining the eyepieces of the flamethrower sight and the NVD, the soldier sees the sight grid and can confidently hit the target, — an officer familiar with the situation explained to Izvestia. At the same time, the editor of the WorldGuns.ru website, Maxim Popenker, told Izvestia that in the West, thermobaric charges are now being abandoned in favor of conventional high-explosive charges in urban combat conditions. — Now, in conditions when battles are mainly fought in urban buildings against terrorist militants, it is very important not to hit the civilian population. A thermobaric charge is very effective, but it causes such destruction that civilians in neighboring houses may suffer. Therefore, now the world trend is becoming conventional high-explosive charges for grenade launchers, capable of breaking through the wall of a building and destroying militants inside. Thus, minimizing damage and casualties, — the expert explained..

A few days ago, Deputy Prime Minister D. Rogozin visited the 106th Guards Airborne Division's training ground in the Tula Region. During this visit, many events took place and a large number of statements were made, but the most interesting words and deeds were those concerning anti-tank missile systems and their accompanying systems. The events surrounding the anti-tank systems began with the Deputy Prime Minister being given a short course in using the Metis-M1 system. After the briefing, Rogozin tested his acquired knowledge on a computer simulator. Then followed a training launch of a missile from a combat training missile system, which differs from a full-fledged combat missile system only by the presence of a second optical system for the instructor. After a short training session, the Deputy Prime Minister, under the guidance of instructor G. Epifanov, hit the training target - an old tank with a white dot painted on it. As it turned out a little later, Rogozin managed to hit the mark on the armor from a distance of two kilometers. According to the Deputy Prime Minister, shooting from the Metis-M1 anti-tank system turned out to be surprisingly easy. As is known, Rogozin is engaged in shooting, but before that he had only dealt with small arms. Mastering the anti-tank system was very simple: after a short briefing and a short practice on a computer simulator, he managed to hit the target. Rogozin noted that the Metis is easy to operate and is quite accessible for mastering in a year of service. Therefore, conscripts should not have problems using such systems. Unfortunately for the Deputy Prime Minister, he managed to launch only one missile. At that time, pilots participating in the Victory Parade were training in the sky above the range, and therefore all firing was temporarily suspended. However, such bans are the exception rather than the rule. According to the range commander, Guards Captain S. Yakushkin, soldiers fire at the range almost every day and no one skimps on ammunition. The fighters learn to use the available weapons both in theory and in practice. And yet, the range commander complained about the insufficient number of new weapons systems. A peculiar illustration of the words about constant training was one of the exhibits of the improvised exhibition organized for the arrival of the high-ranking guest. In addition to armored combat vehicles for the landing force, the site featured an armored car "Tiger" with a combat module installed on it. In the cargo volume of the armored car, two lifting structures are installed, on which launchers of anti-tank missiles "Kornet-EM" are fixed. Each launcher has its own devices for aiming and guidance, and can also simultaneously carry four transport and launch containers with missiles. Thus, eight missiles are located simultaneously on both launchers of this modification of the Tiger. The same number is located inside the vehicle and, if necessary, can be placed on the launchers. Deputy General Director of the Tula Instrument Design Bureau, which developed the anti-tank missiles, N. Khokhlov said that the combat vehicle crew can prepare them for firing and attach them to the launchers without leaving the vehicle. To do this, the launchers are lowered into the lightly armored hull. Of particular interest are the words of the Commander-in-Chief of the Ground Forces, Colonel General V. Chirkin, concerning the effectiveness of the Tiger vehicle with Kornet-EM anti-tank missiles. According to him, one unit of such equipment can destroy up to 16 enemy tanks, due to which eight vehicles have the same effectiveness as an anti-tank artillery division armed with guns like the MT-12 Rapira. Thus, just ten anti-tank systems on a wheeled chassis are enough to ensure the defense of an entire motorized rifle brigade. Rogozin commented on the capabilities of the Tiger with missiles a little differently: in the opinion of the Deputy Prime Minister, one vehicle is capable of fighting "with a tank company at once." In addition, he noted the high export potential of the proposed modification of the armored vehicle. As for the specific running characteristics of the wheeled armored vehicle, V. Chirkin is inclined to evaluate them as an advantage. As an example, he cited the famous march in Kosovo. In his opinion, this march on new armored vehicles would take three times less time than on armored personnel carriers, as was the case in the late nineties. Data The existence of the Kornet family anti-tank system based on the Tiger armored vehicle became known several years ago. It was first demonstrated at the MAKS-2011 international exhibition. At the same time, the characteristics of the system were announced. Thus, the Kornet-EM missiles are capable of hitting targets at ranges from 150 meters to 10 kilometers. The missile is guided to the target automatically, using a laser beam. The presence of two launchers with their own sighting devices and a laser guidance system allows the combat vehicle to simultaneously launch and guide two missiles, which can be sent to one target or to different ones. The sighting equipment of the Kornet-EM missile system allows finding and firing at targets not only during the day, but also at night. The sight has two channels: a daytime television channel and a nighttime thermal imaging channel. Each channel can operate in two modes: wide-field and narrow-field, which differ from each other in the size of the observed sector. After detecting a target, the anti-tank system operator can measure the distance to it using a laser rangefinder. It is noteworthy that the range of measured distances significantly exceeds the radius of action of missiles: it allows measuring distances up to 15 kilometers. After detection, the target is tracked automatically or manually. According to data from 2011, the Kornet-EM system could use two types of missiles, with a tandem cumulative and volume-detonating warhead. The missiles have a high degree of unification, but differ in some characteristics. For example, a missile with an armor-piercing warhead together with a transport and launch container weighs 31 kilograms, i.e. two kilograms less than a munition with a thermobaric one. In addition, the anti-tank missile flies a little slower, at a speed of 300 m/s versus 320 m/s. It is worth noting that the most important difference between the missiles is their range. The anti-tank munition flies only eight kilometers, and the range of ten kilometers applies to a missile with a thermobaric charge. According to official data, a missile with a tandem cumulative warhead is capable of penetrating up to 1100-1300 millimeters of homogeneous armor behind dynamic protection. The action of the thermobaric warhead of another Kornet-EM munition, in turn, is equivalent to the explosion of seven kilograms of TNT. Opinions Back in 2011, the Tiger with Kornet missiles became the subject of heated discussions. The proposed combat vehicle had both supporters and opponents. In recent days, due to the new appearance of the anti-tank complex on a wheeled base, the discussions have flared up with renewed vigor. Both sides of the debate put forward their arguments and defend their point of view in every possible way. The main advantage of the self-propelled anti-tank complex is its large ammunition supply. Unlike infantrymen armed with such missiles, the armored vehicle is capable of transporting 16 missiles of both types at once. Thus, as the commander-in-chief of the ground forces and the deputy prime minister noted, one combat vehicle in ideal conditions can destroy up to 16 enemy tanks at a distance significantly exceeding the capabilities of enemy tank weapons. The firing range of the most modern tank guns is several times less than the maximum range of the Kornet-EM, and not all existing tanks can give a symmetrical response in the form of a guided missile launched from the gun barrel. The second advantage of the Tiger with Kornet-EM concerns its mobility. The wheeled chassis provides good performance on the highway and acceptable off-road. Thanks to this, combat vehicles will be able to change their position before enemy tanks, artillery or aircraft strike. Of course, the wheeled chassis by definition cannot provide cross-country ability at the level of tanks, but the lightweight base chassis significantly increases the chances of survival on the battlefield. In addition, the retractable launchers to some extent camouflage the combat vehicle, preventing the enemy from accurately identifying it among other equipment on the same base. The new self-propelled anti-tank complex's armament range includes two types of missiles. Thus, the Tiger with launchers is not "tied" to a specific type of target. If necessary, it can fire at enemy infantry or fortifications with missiles with a volume-detonating warhead. In combination with a relatively large ammunition load, the range of ammunition will allow the new combat vehicles to perform a wide range of tasks. Finally, the combination of characteristics makes the vehicle-based missile system a more promising weapon system than towed anti-tank artillery. In fact, the Kornet-EM is inferior to the Rapier only in terms of ammunition cost. In terms of mobility, survivability and firing range, the missile system is ahead of the cannon. Several decades ago, an attempt was made to increase the potential of the MT-12 cannon. During this work, Tula engineers from the Machine-Building Design Bureau created the 9K116 Kastet system with a 9M117 guided missile launched through the cannon barrel. For its time, this was a worthy example of weapons, but now it has a very serious competitor in the form of the Kornet-EM system. The main problem with the Tiger with anti-tank missiles is the weak protection of the base vehicle. An unarmored or lightly armored vehicle can be damaged or destroyed even by small arms. In addition, vehicles with anti-tank weapons with greater capabilities will become the first target for an enemy strike, which will only worsen the situation with their survival on the battlefield. Finally, the cross-country ability of a wheeled chassis is significantly worse than that of tracked vehicles. Thus, an insufficiently protected self-propelled anti-tank system risks getting stuck on the battlefield and becoming a convenient target for any suitable weapon. The second problem of the anti-tank complex based on the Tiger vehicle, as well as other similar systems, lies in the ratio of characteristics and the real situation. The Kornet-EM armor-piercing missile flies eight kilometers only in ideal proving ground conditions. Not every area where a battle involving tanks and anti-tank systems is possible will allow missile launches at maximum range. Thus, the chances of the anti-tank complex to eliminate enemy tanks and/or helicopters before a retaliatory strike are significantly reduced, depending on the characteristics of the specific landscape. After reducing the maximum visibility range and, as a consequence, shooting, the chances of both sides can be equalized. If necessary, in appropriate conditions, an armored car with missiles can be located on a natural elevation, which will increase its "horizon". But in this case, the combat vehicle will attract the attention of enemy artillery and aviation, which, combined with low survivability, dooms it to destruction. In light of the relatively low survivability of the self-propelled complex and the specific conditions of combat use, the issue of cost comes to the fore. In a number of cases, the accuracy and mobility of the Tiger with missiles will cost too much, especially in comparison with anti-tank artillery. In addition to cheap "blanks", the ammunition of existing guns also includes guided missiles, which under certain conditions can prove to be a worthy replacement for the ammunition of the Kornet-EM complex. Future It is difficult to say what the future holds for the Kornet-EM anti-tank missile system based on the Tigr wheeled chassis. It has both pros and cons that require additional analysis. It is likely that in the future the Russian armed forces will begin to receive such systems en masse. The reason for this development could be D. Rogozin's well-known love for new promising types of weapons and the patronage motivated by it. However, another assumption seems more plausible and practical. According to the Instrument Design Bureau, Kornet-EM missile systems can be mounted on any suitable chassis, regardless of the type of chassis. Therefore, in the very near future, new versions of the anti-tank system may appear, based on armored tracked vehicles. Such equipment already exists and is in service: this is the Shturm-S combat vehicle based on the MT-LB tractor and the Khrizantema system mounted on the BMP-3 chassis. The need for full-scale mass equipping of ground forces with self-propelled anti-tank missile systems has been long overdue. However, the state of the existing fleet of such systems and the pace of its renewal leave much to be desired. It is quite possible that the next modification of the well-deserved Kornet complex will become the very system that will significantly improve the situation in the troops and give them a worthy argument in a possible fight against enemy tank units.

The shortened AKS-74U assault rifle was developed in the late 1970s as part of the "Modern" competition program based on the standard AK-74 assault rifle (in the AKS-74 modification with a folding buttstock) specifically for arming combat vehicle crews, gun crews and other army personnel for whom the standard AK-74 assault rifle is too large. In addition to the army, the AKS-74U is widely used in the Ministry of Internal Affairs, as it is compact and convenient for moving in a car and when operating indoors. For the same reason, and also because it can be easily hidden in a bag or under clothing, the AKS-74U is popular among various security services for high-ranking government officials, as well as among bandits. \n In terms of its dimensions and weight, as well as its tactical niche, the AKS-74U is close to submachine guns (however, due to the intermediate cartridge used and the mechanisms of the standard assault rifle, it is still considered a shortened assault rifle, and not a PP. The advantages of the AKS-74U include high mobility in cramped conditions (rooms, car interiors), the possibility of concealed carry, and a fairly high penetration ability of the cartridge. Disadvantages include a short aiming range of fire (with a fairly large lethal range of the bullet), low stopping power of the bullet, and a tendency to quickly overheat when firing. \n The AKS-74U was created with maximum unification of units and parts with the AK-74 / AKS-74 assault rifles. It differs from the AKS-74 in the following: the barrel is shortened by 2 times with a correspondingly moved back gas chamber, the gas piston rod is shortened; a special a muzzle that serves as an expansion chamber (for reliable operation of the gas engine of the automatic) and a flame arrester; the receiver cover is hinged to the receiver in its front part; a flip-up rear sight, with settings for 200 and 400 meters, is fixed to the receiver cover. All mechanisms, controls and the general design of the AKS-74U are similar to the design of the AKS-74 assault rifle.

AKS 74 is a variant of AK 74 with a pentagonal metal butt that folds to the side. It was created for use in airborne troops (an assault rifle with a non-folding butt cannot be conveniently and safely placed in a parachute suspension system).

AK-74M — AK-74 modernized. Equipped with a side-folding plastic buttstock and a universal mount (dovetail rail) formounts for sights, both optical and night, on the left side of the receiver. Thus, the AK74M replaced four models at once: AK74, AKS74, AK74N and AKS74N. The handguard and the barrel pad of the gas tube are made of impact-resistant glass-filled polyamide AG-4V. The changes also affected the muzzle brake, which received open chambers, which allows it to be cleaned without removing it. To reduce the likelihood of mechanical damage to the receiver cover, the latter was significantly strengthened. A lock appeared in the design of the guide rod of the return spring, which allows firing from the GP-25 or GP-30 underbarrel grenade launcher without using an additional mount for the receiver cover, which is necessary in such cases for the AK74. With the new assault rifles, it is possible to use the PK-A and PK-01 collimator sights. In 1991, the AK 74M was accepted into service and its serial production began at the Izhevsk Machine-Building Plant.

The Kalashnikov AK-101 assault rifle is an export modification of the standard Russian Army assault rifle AK-74M. The main (and actually the only) difference between the AK-101 and the AK-74M is the caliber - the "hundredth" series assault rifle uses 5.56mm (5.56x45 NATO) cartridges and corresponding magazines, otherwise the assault rifles are identical.

The Kalashnikov AK-103 assault rifle is an export modification of the standard Russian Army assault rifle AK-74M.
