The HK MP7A1 automatic system is built according to the scheme with a gas engine with a short stroke of the gas piston, locking - by rotating the bolt. In general, the automatic system and USMHK MP7A1 strongly resemble the corresponding elements of the G36 rifle of the same company. The HKMP7A1 body is made of plastic, on top of the body there are guides for mounting various sighting devices. Fire modes are single shots and bursts, the fire mode switch also acts as a safety and is controlled from both sides of the weapon. The cocking handle is located on the back of the body above the butt, has a T-shape (like the cocking handle of the M16 rifle) and is stationary when firing. Fire is conducted from a closed bolt.

When creating this submachine gun, the goal was to maximize the accuracy of automatic fire, while maintaining the small dimensions and weight of the weapon and using fairly powerful .45 ACP cartridges, which are distinguished by a high stopping effect at short distances. The Kriss Super V submachine gun uses a rather unusual automatic scheme with a semi-free breech and redirection of the recoil impulse of the moving parts downwards. The fairly light breech of the submachine gun has side projections that enter the inclined grooves of a massive balancer that moves up and down inside the weapon body. When firing, the backward movement of the breech is converted into a vertical downward movement of the balancer, while due to the variable angle of the groove along which the breech projections slide, it is slowed down at an early stage of the breech recoil. Shooting is carried out from a closed breech, the hammer firing mechanism is located above the barrel axis. The cocking handle is folding, located on the left side of the weapon body and remains stationary during firing. The fire mode selector is located on the trigger mechanism box approximately in the middle of the weapon's length, and provides firing of single shots and bursts. A separate safety is located above the pistol grip. To increase the stability of the weapon and further reduce the barrel toss when firing without using the butt, the pistol grip is raised relative to the barrel bore axis so that the recoil force shoulder relative to the hand holding the weapon is minimal (a similar solution was previously used in the Soviet Olympic small-caliber pistol MTs-3, also known as the "Sheptarsky frame"). The magazine receiver is located in front of the fire control handle. Currently, 13-round magazines from Glock 21 pistols are used, but in the future it is planned to develop our own magazines with a higher capacity. To improve the accuracy of fire, the Kriss Super V submachine gun is equipped with a side-folding butt and a removable front grip.

The PP-19 Bison submachine gun was designed in the early 1990s at the Izhevsk Machine-Building Plant with the aim of arming the Ministry of Internal Affairs with modern compact automatic small arms to replace the AKS-74U. At the same time, the PP-19 itself was developed and produced with a large volume of parts from the Kalashnikov AK-74 assault rifle. These parts include the shortened AK-74 receiver with a pistol grip for controlling fire and the trigger mechanism, as well as a metal folding buttstock borrowed from the AKS-74 assault rifle. The PP-19 Bison submachine gun is distinguished by its original auger magazine with a capacity of 64 rounds for the PP-19 Bison-2 chambered for 9x18 PM cartridges and 53 rounds for the PP-19 Bison-2-01 chambered for 9x19 cartridges.Parabellum. This magazine was created partly on the basis of the American submachine gun Calico. At the same time, the PP-19 magazine is placed on the weapon with great convenience for the shooter and combines the function of the fore-end. The magazine is located under the barrel, does not increase the dimensions of the submachine gun and performs the function of the fore-end for holding the weapon when firing. For ease of carrying and transportation, the submachine gun has a folding butt. There are two versions of the butt - a frame butt, folding to the left side or an upper butt, folding on the cover of the receiver. The design of the submachine gun allows firing with a folded butt. The Bison is perfectly adapted for use in combination with silent shooting devices and modern fire control systems. A side bar is provided for installing collimator, optical and night sights.

The PP-2000 submachine gun was developed by the Instrument-Making Design Bureau (KBP) in Tula, Russia, and was first shown to the public in 2004, although a patent for its design was registered back in 2001. The PP-2000 is clearly intended to serve as either a personal defense weapon for military personnel (PDW) or as a close combat weapon for special operations forces, both military and police/militia, primarily for action in urban environments. The PP-2000 is extremely compact and lightweight, with a minimum number of parts and a simple design, ensuring easy operation and low cost. The ability to use high-power armor-piercing ammunition 7N21 and 7N31, originally developed for the GSh-18 pistol, allows the PP-2000 to be used against opponents in personal protective equipment (helmets, bulletproof vests), and to effectively hit targets inside cars. At the same time, compared to small-caliber analogues produced in Western countries, such as the Belgian 5.7mm FN P90 or the German 4.6mm HK MP-7, the PP-2000, thanks to the use of 9mm bullets, provides greater effectiveness against targets not protected by bulletproof vests. Currently, the PP-2000 is already in serial production and is entering service with the Russian Ministry of Internal Affairs. The PP-2000 submachine gun is based on a free-blowback mechanism. Fire from the PP-2000 is conducted from a closed bolt, the firing mechanism is hammer. The submachine gun body is made of plastic as a single piece with a pistol grip and an enlarged trigger guard, which allows holding the weapon with two hands if necessary. The bolt protrudes from the body above the barrel, and a cocking handle that can be deflected to the right or left is installed in its front part. The magazine is inserted into the pistol grip, the magazine release button is located at the base of the trigger guard. The safety selector is located on the left side of the weapon, above the pistol grip, and allows firing both single shots and bursts. A distinctive feature of the PP-2000, protected by a patent, is in the rear partIts body has a slot for a spare magazine. When a magazine is inserted into this slot, it can be used as a rudimentary shoulder rest.

The PP-19-01 Vityaz submachine gun is a further development of the PP-19 Bizon submachine gun. The Vityaz was developed by the IZHMASH concern specifically to meet the requirements of the Vityaz special forces unit of the Russian Ministry of Internal Affairs, from which it got its name. Currently, the PP-19-01 Vityaz submachine gun is in serial production and is already entering service with units of the Russian Ministry of Internal Affairs. Like its predecessor, the PP-19-01 design is based on the AKS-74U assault rifle or the newer AK-104 assault rifle, with which the Vityaz shares about 70% of its parts. The Vityaz is available in two versions - the basic PP-19-01 ver.10 and the modified PP-19-01 ver.20 Vityaz-SN. The second version has improved ergonomics (due to the safety catch and bolt handle being moved to the left side), as well as an additional Picatinny rail on the receiver cover, allowing for the installation of various additional sighting devices. The PP-19-01 Vityaz submachine gun can use most commercial and military versions of the 9x19 cartridge produced in the world, including domestic 9mm 7N21 cartridges with an armor-piercing bullet. The PP-19-01 can be equipped with a shot silencer. The PP-19-01 Vityaz submachine gun uses a free-bolt mechanism. Fire is conducted from a closed bolt, the trigger and safety mechanisms, together with the receiver, are completely borrowed from the AKS-74U assault rifle. Attached to the bottom of the receiver is a shaft for box double-row magazines, developed specifically for the PP-19-01. If necessary, the magazines can be fastened in pairs using a special bracket supplied with the weapon, ensuring rapid reloading of the weapon in combat. The butt is frame, metal, folding to the side. On the left side of the receiver is a standard for domestic weapons bar for mounting brackets for optical sights. The sight is sector, open, adjustable for range.

The Minimi machine gun was developed by the Belgian company FN Herstal in the mid- to late 1970s and early 1980s and has been in serial production since about 1981. It is in service with many countries, including theBelgium, the USA (designated as the M249 SAW), Canada (designated as the C9), Australia (designated as the F-89) and many others. The machine gun is deservedly popular for its high mobility combined with firepower, which significantly exceeds the firepower of such light machine guns as the RPK-74, L86A1 and others, built on the basis of assault rifles, and not created "from scratch" as machine guns. A distinctive feature of the Minimi is the ability to use both a metal belt (standard method) and NATO standard rifle magazines (from the M16 rifle, a backup option) for shooting without any changes in the design (a similar capability was possessed by the Czech Vz.52 light machine gun, created 30 years earlier). Minimi machine guns are used to increase the firepower of infantry squads, providing effective fire at ranges of up to 600-800 meters, combined with high mobility. Minimi is a light (hand-held) machine gun, based on gas-operated automation, the barrel is locked by turning the bolt. It is fed by a metal disintegrating belt or box magazines (the magazine receiver is located on the left side of the weapon under the belt receiver, the magazine is inserted at an angle of approximately 45 degrees down from the horizontal). When using a belt, the magazine receiver window is covered with a dust curtain, when inserting the magazine (with the belt removed), the open curtain blocks the belt feed path. When using a belt, part of the gas engine energy is spent on pulling the belt, so with a belt the rate of fire is lower than with magazine feeding. The belt is usually fed from plastic boxes or canvas "bags" on a metal frame, adjacent to the machine gun from below, with a capacity of 100 or 200 rounds. The machine gun barrel is quick-changeable, equipped with a flame arrester and a folding carrying handle. Three main barrel sizes are available - standard 465mm long, "paratroop" 349mm long and "special purpose" 406mm long. The bipod is folding and located under the barrel on the gas outlet tube. Depending on the country of manufacture and modification, Minimi can have butts and forends of various designs, mounts for optical and night sights, etc. Fire control - using a pistol grip with a trigger, fire mode - only automatic.

PKM machine guns are highly reliable and deservedly popular among the troops, despite the somewhat overcomplicated two-stage system of feeding cartridges from the belt into the barrel. The Kalashnikov machine gun uses gas-operated automation with a gas piston with a long working stroke located under the barrel. The barrel is quick-changeable, has a carrying handle, also used to replace the hot barrel. The gas-operated unit is equipped with a manual gas regulator. The barrel is locked by turning the bolt. Cartridges are fed from a non-disintegrating metal belt with a closed link. Belts are assembled from pieces of 50 links using a cartridge. The standard capacity of belts is 100 (in the manual version) or 200 (in the easel) cartridges. The direction of belt feed is from right to left, the windows for feeding and exiting the belt are equipped with dust covers, as is the window forejection of spent cartridges. The feed of cartridges from the belt is two-stage - first, a special grip pulls the cartridge from the belt back when the bolt frame rolls back, after which the cartridge is lowered to the chambering line and is chambered into the barrel when the bolt rolls back. Shooting is carried out from an open bolt, only with automatic fire. Standard controls in the infantry version include a pistol grip, trigger, manual safety and frame butt. In the armored personnel carrier version, it is possible to install a special butt plate with twin handles and a trigger key instead of a butt, in the tank version, an electric remote trigger mechanism is used. In the infantry version, the machine gun is equipped with a folding bipod, in the easel version, a universal tripod with an adapter for anti-aircraft fire is additionally used. The standard sighting devices are open, infantry versions with a side bracket for installing night sights were also produced.

In the mid-1950s, the Soviet Army began a program to develop a new small arms system designed to replace the Kalashnikov AK assault rifle, SKS carbine, and RPD light machine gun. The system was to include an assault rifle and a light machine gun (squad support weapon) that was as unified as possible with it, both chambered for the 7.62x39 M43 cartridge. Following a competition in 1961, the SA adopted a modified Kalashnikov AKM assault rifle and a Kalashnikov RPK light machine gun, unified with it in design and magazines. The RPK remained the main squad support weapon until 1974, when it was replaced by its counterpart chambered for the 5.45x39 cartridge - the RPK-74 light machine gun. The Kalashnikov RPK light machine gun uses the same automatic system and basic design solutions as the Kalashnikov AKM assault rifle, i.e. gas-operated automatics with barrel locking by rotating the bolt. The receiver is stamped from sheet steel, more durable compared to the AKM receiver to increase the service life. The barrel is elongated compared to the AKM, it does not have the ability to be replaced in case of overheating. The trigger mechanism is completely similar to that of the AKM, allows firing single shots and bursts, firing is carried out from a closed bolt. Cartridges are fed from detachable magazines compatible with AK / AKM assault rifles. Two types of high-capacity magazines were additionally developed and put into service for the RPK - a box (horn) magazine for 40 rounds and a drum magazine for 75 rounds. Early versions of box magazines were made of steel, later ones - of plastic. The drum magazines had a steel construction and were expensive and slow to load with cartridges. The RPK was equipped with a folding bipod mounted under the barrel, a specially shaped buttstock and a sight with the ability to make lateral adjustments. The RPKS variant, developed for airborne troops, had a side-folding buttstock. In addition, the RPKN and RPKSN variants were produced with a bar mounted on the receiver for attaching night sights. Currently, the RPK-74M is used as the basis for the RPKM machine gun chambered for 7.62x39, intended primarily for export. It should be noted that as a light machine gun, the RPK had significant drawbacks - low capacity of the power supply system, the inability to conduct intensive automatic fire due to the non-removable barrel and firing from a closed bolt. Its main advantage was the high degree of unification with the standard AKM assault rifle, and somewhat greater firing range and accuracy compared to it (due to a longer and somewhat heavier barrel).

RPG-7V1, hand-held anti-tank grenade launcher. Designed to combat tanks, self-propelled artillery units. The grenade launcher is based on a recoilless reusable launcher and a shot with an over-caliber warhead. The grenade launcher has a widening of the barrel in its middle part - a charging chamber - for more complete use of the energy of the propellant charge, and a bell in the breech - to ensure recoillessness of the complex. In addition to the mechanical sight, the grenade launcher has an optical sight. The constant development of new rounds with grenades of various damaging effects for the grenade launcher, the improvement of sighting devices significantly expands the capabilities of the grenade launcher, making it multipurpose.

The RG-6 grenade launcher (GRAU index 6G30) was developed in a hurry in 1993 at the TsKIB SOO (Central Design Bureau of Sporting and Hunting Weapons, Tula) to arm troops operating against separatists in Chechnya. Small-scale production of the RG-6 began in 1994 at the Tula Arms Plant, and the grenade launcher almost immediately began to be supplied to troops and some units of the Ministry of Internal Affairs. Due to the haste of development, early-production grenade launchers had some problems, in particular, unreliable trigger mechanism operation. The RG-6 grenade launcher was undoubtedly influenced by the South African Milkor MGL grenade launcher, but due toIn addition to the fact that the RG-6 uses fundamentally different ammunition with a flying cartridge case, there are also a number of noticeable differences in its design.\n The RG-6 grenade launcher has a revolver design, the drum chambers are formed by six rifled muzzle-loading barrels from the GP-25 grenade launchers. Accordingly, the drum is loaded from the front, after the front part of the grenade launcher with the false barrel is folded to the side and up. The grenades are held in the chambers by special spring clamps. The drum rotates with a spring, which is cocked manually during the reloading process. The drum rotates after each shot when the ratchet is released at the moment the trigger is released, which eliminates the possibility of re-firing a grenade that has misfired. In addition, in the case of a delayed shot, the misfired grenade immediately "leaves" the barrel, and the author knows of at least one case when a delayed shot in the RG-6 led to the destruction of the weapon (fortunately, the shooter threw the grenade launcher aside in time and no one was hurt). The trigger mechanism is self-cocking, mainly borrowed from the GP-25 grenade launcher. The grenade launcher is equipped with a false barrel with a smooth (non-rifled) channel and serves only to attach the front handle and sight. The sights are folding, stepped type. The buttstock is sliding telescopic, has a rubber shock-absorbing butt plate.

RGD-5 (hand grenade, remote, GRAU index - 57-G-717) - Soviet offensive hand grenade, belongs to anti-personnel fragmentation hand grenades of remote action of the offensive type. This means that it is designed to defeat enemy personnel with fragments of the hull during its explosion. The grenade reaches its target by throwing it by hand. Remote action - means that the grenade will explode after a certain time (3.2-4.2 seconds) after it is released, regardless of other conditions. Offensive type - means that the grenade fragments have a small mass and fly at a distance shorter than the possible throwing range.\n Externally, the grenade has an oval body made of thin steel. The streamlined body is assembled from the upper and lower parts, each of which includes an outer shell and an insert. The opening for the fuse is closed with a plastic plug during storage. The mass of the grenade with the fuse is 310 g. The explosive charge is TNT weighing 110 grams. The range of fragments is 30 meters.\n The fuse for the grenade is universal, also suitable for the RG-42 and F-1 grenades. The fuse brand is: UZRG, UZRGM (since the second half of the 1950s), or UZRGM-2. All these fuses are interchangeable.

F-1 (GRAU index - 57-G-721) - a hand-held anti-personnel defensive grenade. Designed to defeat manpower in a defensive battle. Due to the significant radius of fragmentation, it can only be thrown from behind cover, from an armored personnel carrier or from a tank.\n The names "F-1" and the slang "limonka" come from the French F-1 fragmentation grenade model 1915 weighing 572 grams and the English Lemon grenade, which were supplied to Russia during World War I. Another version of the origin of the slang name is its shape, reminiscent of a lemon. \n Initially, F-1 grenades were equipped with a fuse by F. V. Koveshnikov. In 1941, E. M. Vitseni and A. A. Bednyakov developed a universal fuse UZRG, after the war it was modified and serves to this day under the name UZRGM (universal fuse for hand grenades modernized).

The modern American hand grenade, commonly referred to as a concussion grenade, is designed to inflict casualties primarily through the force of its blast. It also inflicts casualties through fragmentation, but the fragmentation plays a secondary role.\n The main task of this grenade is to cause destruction (damage to unarmored or lightly armored vehicles, small structures (dugouts, crevices, rooms, fuel tanks, containers with property) and to incapacitate enemy soldiers located in a confined space (in vehicles, shelters, dugouts, rooms). A significant high explosive effect is achieved due to the large mass of explosive charge (TNT) in the grenade - 227 grams with a total mass of the grenade of 443 grams. The length of the grenade is 13.8 cm, the diameter is about 5 cm. According to American standards, the radius of fatal injury to a person by a shock wave is 2 meters. Fragments of the grenade can fly up to 200 meters. The radius of safe removal of your soldiers is 235 meters. The fuses used are M206A1 or M206A2. The grenade is remote-action, i.e. the explosion occurs 4-6 seconds after the release of the pressure lever. Before throwing, the soldier presses the lever to the grenade body, pulls out the safety ring and throws the grenade at the target. The grenade can only be thrown from behind cover.\n The maximum throwing range of an average soldier is considered to be 40 meters. The grenade is painted black. Yellow markings are on the side of the grenade. This grenade gained great popularity during the Vietnam War in the sixties, when American soldiers used them to destroy the Viet Cong hiding in underground passages. This could not be done with ordinary grenades. The grenade may have some effectiveness in street fighting, mountain conditions. In a regular field battle, its effectiveness is questionable.

The M33 offensive grenade was developed as a grenade that would be convenient for accurate throwing. The grenade has a spherical body with an internal notch. The M33 is loaded with composition "B" as a charge. When the grenade explodes, about 400 fragments are formed. The M33 grenade used the M204 series fuse. Based on the M33 grenade, its modification was developed - the M67 grenade, which differed from the M67 only in the fuse used. The M67 grenade uses the M213 fuse, which differs from the standard one in having two safety devices - a regular pin with a ring and a wire loop wrapped around the fuse and holding the lever. Due to the reliable safety, which allows you to carry the grenade by hooking the fuse lever onto the equipment belt, as well as small size with a high density of fragments in the affected area, the M67 grenade was popular with paratroopers, marines and special forces. \n A further modification was the M68 grenade, similar to the M59, which was equipped with an M217 fuse, but with a wire loop wrapped around the fuse and holding the lever, in order to increase safety. For training in the rules of use, a imitation grenade M69 is used, repeating the shape and weight of the M67 grenade. In South Korea, a copy of the M67 grenade with a modified fuse design is produced under the designation K400.

"Griffin found me in Staroselye. The local basement dive on the outskirts of an abandoned village welcomed anyone and everyone, drawing everyone who came there into its alcoholic abyss with cheap vodka and cigarette smoke mixed with marijuana. It was already the second week of a deep binge, the money was running out, and I understood that when-someday this unconscious state will finally chew me up and spit me out into the surrounding reality. It was then that Griff sat down at my table, along with a bottle of vodka, two poorly washed glasses and a plump folder. "They" had everything. All the information about me before the Zone, everything about the events of two months ago on Rassokha. The griffin had me by the balls, so I had to agree to all his conditions. It turns out that "they" had their eye on me for a long time. Apparently, they needed "personnel" like me no less than other groups involved in dividing up territory and loot. Moreover, they needed me so much that "they" managed to find me even here, in this hole. Later, I tried to get all the available information about this organization, but I only managed to get the name - the Syndicate. What it was - a criminal group, a united cartel of mercenaries, someone's small victorious private army - I couldn't find out. The case that Griff proposed seemed like a piece of cake from his words and at first glance, but the inner voice that I had grown accustomed to trust sang at the top of my lungs - "Man, it's not just that there's something fried here, everything's burned to hell!" All it took was to get to the southern Marshes of the left bank of Pripyat, dig up information on a certain group called "Clear Sky", find their base and destroy every surviving member of this clan. Well, I'll have to sweat and run around so that the Syndicate forgets about my existence, pays the promised fee and gives me a new identity there, on the mainland. Griff didn't even argue with my terms, he simply nodded his head, said "You'll get the summary information in ten days, at this same table" and left. And my inner voice became completely hoarse and silent."

"A vulture is a scavenger bird. One mercenary I know lives up to his nickname 100%. Indeterminate age (35-50), large build, unremarkable face, no special features. Trained, first-class fighter. His brains are fine too. Calculating, cold-blooded, deadly dangerous. Probably, in the past he was in the state special services, then retrained as an intruder - I can smell "my own", that is, hired killers, a mile away. I managed to find out the employer - a certain Syndicate, a criminal organization about which, to my surprise, I could not get a single gram of sensible information. For now - we dance with what we have."

"The griffin sent a message: "Your legend is the free stalker Red, from the east coast. After an ambush by a gang of marauders, in which your entire group died,started working for scientists from the MNI ChAZO. The last contract was to confirm information about the appearance of previously unknown to science anomalies and artifacts in the southern Swamps, conduct preliminary data collection, measure key indicators, meet the main group, and provide security and escort. You will work through one stalker who owes us money, he is digging up information on the Black Angel - a completely new type of anomaly that recently appeared in the southwest of the Zone. Call sign - Czech. A guide nicknamed Gray will help you find him in the Swamps. He is a freelancer. After the job is done, I recommend eliminating him. The Czech knows that you work for us, but he doesn't need to know the real purpose of your visit. I advise using him in the dark - to help with the anomalies, through him to determine the location of the Clear Sky base and eliminate the surviving Clear Sky members. In case of information leakage, carry out a purge. You know what's at stake. Don't let us down."

"Sedoy is no longer young, about 45-50 years old, a stalker, lives in the Zone, earns his living by hunting and by "guiding" other stalkers and other foreign tourists. The business is not as profitable as hunting for artifacts or working on contracts for scientists, but it is safer. In his business, Sedoy is considered an expert - most of his routes in the south of the Zone are relatively safe, among the people who went with him, there were few deaths, and his reputation among stalkers has developed a positive one. Mainly due to his principle of not getting involved with marauders and mercenaries - for some reason of his own, he fiercely hates all this fraternity. One can only guess how Griff managed to agree with Sedoy about me. Hard cash played a role here, or everything was done through front men, but working with an experienced guide who knows the Swamps like the back of his hand removed a number of problematic issues."

"All the facts point to the recruitment of a squad of mercenaries to eliminate every stalker who accidentally or intentionally found himself connected to the Swamps. I doubt that Gray and the Glock he mentioned knew that I would be in the Swamps, andSo there is no direct connection to me. There is only one option left - Griff and his vaunted Syndicate have leaked something somewhere. It remains unclear what exactly this is connected with - the study of anomalies like the Black Angel, or the search for the Clear Sky base. And if the first is connected with me indirectly, then the second is directly. It is necessary to inform Griff about this at the next communication session."

"The guide clearly wasn't planning on going to the other world anytime soon, which means something made him commit suicide. Or someone. I'm sure the corpses of the mercenaries who surrounded me in the cave were the work of the same hands that drove Sedy to suicide - one of them could have survived the shootout with me and told me what the guide didn't have time to tell. "Or maybe it's some unknown friend who wanted to save my skin and remain incognito. Either way, I have absolutely no desire to get involved with a force that can casually force several people to shoot themselves at the same time."

"Before his death, the blundering guide managed to tell me about the impending attack, which greatly helped me survive. Information about his acquaintance and employer Glock may also be useful in the future."
"I shouldn't have decided to pull off this adventure with a diversionary maneuver. I had to reveal myself to the mercenaries, but the guide will definitely not tell anyone anything now."

"The stalker camp greeted me with sullen indifference. Everyone was busy with their own business - someone was resting after a grueling hike, someone was arguing with a local huckster, and someone was practicing shooting at the most harmless targets in the Zone - crows. The stalker, watching the passage among the anomalies leading to the camp, demonstratively took the Saiga off safety and nodded towards a burly fellow standing in the shadow of an awning and watching those sitting in a circle of fire. - What's your name? - The guy didn't even turn around in my direction, continuing to look at the fire. - Red. - The locals call me Werewolf. A loner? - Yes. At the request of scientists from Yantar, I want to conduct a series of measurements of the new anomaly. - The Tadpoles are interested in the Black Angel? Hmm.. - Werewolf turned around, looking at me with interest. - They don't work with greenhorns. I'm sure you know the rules. We won't have any problems? - No. - There was no point in lying, even if problems arose, none of the locals would survive them. But it was unwise to talk about it directly. - Okay. Make yourself at home. If you need work, just ask. The conversation was over, the taciturn leader of the local crowd lost interest in me and again stared at the fire. The acquaintance took place. Grif described Czech's appearance to me in detail, so it won't be difficult to figure him out."

“The leader of the free stalkers in the Swamps, a taciturn, self-confident big guy nicknamed Werewolf, didn’t appeal to me at first sight. I sawThere are plenty of tramps in their time, but this stalker gave the impression of a man "in a mask", as if he was hiding a part of his past that many of the locals would not like. Perhaps I noticed this only because Werewolf and I were similar in some ways. But, no matter how you look at it, he was the main one among these stalkers, knew everything they knew, and could help me fulfill the contract, even if he did not suspect it. "

"Grif described this stalker as a calculating and cunning rogue, not without a fair share of talent in the art of shooting. The latter, it seems, more than once allowed Czech to get out of sticky situations alive and well, which could not be said about his enemies. According to rumors, before leaving for freelance work, Czech worked as an informant in one group with three cool stalker specialists - he brought together customers, maintained contact with the mainland, traded information. I even knew one of his partners personally, I had a chance to work together on one hot case. Ghost - I think that was his name. A sniper from God, and he was no fool to carouse. Too bad I haven't heard anything about him for a long time. However, nothing surprising - spend so much time in the hole. Now I had to make up for lost time and fill the information vacuum. If the opportunity arises, I will have to ask Czech about Ghost. If everything that Griff said and what I heard myself is true, then it is unlikely that we will be able to lead this craftsman by the nose to other people's secrets for long. Perhaps liquidation will be necessary."

"A new unknown variable in the equation. This complicates the task. The fact that the Dolgovites were specifically looking for "marked" ones in the Swamps, captured one ofcommanders of Freedom and then were attacked - hardly a coincidence. Especially considering that it was the commander of the Fremen squad who turned out to be tattooed. What could this mean? Just a meaningless tattoo? Conspirators among the Fremen? Then why would Duty need them? Doesn't add up. A new sect? Probably. Freedom members have always been free-thinking and independent. And if the "marked ones" have somehow annoyed the Duty members, they automatically fell into the latter's "zone of interest". Hmm.. Whatever one may say, fortune-telling on tea leaves will not yield results. It's too early to tell Griff, and Czech doesn't need to know about the marked Freedom member yet."

"The Freeman nicknamed Shkaf is helping the cause even after death. His personal PDA and the last message from the commander of another squad will help infiltrate the Fremen. In addition to the legend "for free stalkers", Grif provided me with the necessary cover in case of working with Freedom - freelance guides and fighters are not uncommon for them. Barin's PDA will serve as an additional support. Of course, provided that the Freemen do not shoot indiscriminately upon contact. It's a pity that such an infiltration into Duty is impossible."

"There is fresh information for you. We compared Czech's data on the situation in the Swamps with the current situation of the large clans of the western coast and found a number of discrepancies. The leaders of the "Freedom" group got involved in a conflict between a large association of free veteran stalkers and a gang of marauders nicknamed Borov, on the side of the former. The informant reported that despite all this, the command of the group decided to send one field detachment to find out the details of the "Clear Sky" clan and the circumstances of its disappearance. A certain Goryan, one of the field commanders, volunteered. At the same time, the newly-minted leader of the Fremen, Lukasz, did not even consider other candidates. We checked Goryan's past. We were able to dig up little - he joined Freedom as a volunteer, before joining he had already been in the Zone for 2-2.5 years, an experienced stalker. As a Freeman, he worked as a scout, and then as a commander of a reconnaissance squad - he and his fighters combed everything south of the Brain Scorcher, even visited the eastern shore. He probably received some data on Clear Sky from the command, or he himself had contact with the Clear Sky members during his "wanderings" - it is impossible to track something like that in the Zone. After the conflict with the Regents began, Clear Sky could no longer hide its presence in the ChAZO, and therefore could well have turned to other large clans for help, such as Duty or Freedom. I advise you to find out why Goryan and his squad were sent to the Swamps, and what they really want to find. As for the second squad, everything is much more interesting. The Shkaf squad, like Goryan's, also worked on reconnaissance functions, but unlike the latter, they were engaged in exploring new territories, hunting rare and exotic mutants, and working out the routes of squads of military stalkers and scientists. They went far to the west and northwest. They disappeared without a trace three months ago, the last detected signal was northeast of Limansk. Since then, no one has heard anything about them. And now they "surfaced" in the Swamps, pursued by Duty squads, without contacting their own and without joining Goryan's squad. I recommend finding out the reasons for the appearance of the Shkaf squad in the Swamps and the tasks it was pursuing."

"Goryan is another Svoboda commander. According to information from Grif, he started out as a freelance stalker, and has been in the Zone for several years, which means he has plenty of experience. You can't fool someone like him with chaff, so I'll have to act carefully. I'll have to rework the cover for Shkaf and his squad - I'm still the same freelance stalker in the service of Svoboda, who found the corpses of Svoboda members hidden in the basement on the northern farm. If Goryan is not a fool, he'll make contact at least to find out what else I know."

"Now I have the commander of the Svoboda squad on the hook. Yes, I'll have to help him with the investigation of the causes of the Svoboda deaths, or at least simulate this help, but it's worth it. Especially since Goryan transparently hinted at cooperation in searching for traces of "Clear Sky" if I help him with this. He knows much more than he says, but now it's not so important. There is no benefit in eliminating the Svoboda members for now. At the moment, my goal is to work out the chain of events on the "Indians". Czech's informant spoke of several squads. I doubt that all of them are hunting exclusively for the Svoboda members. Besides, if Goryan considers the Dolgovites to be rivals in the search, it means that they, too, may have information regarding the Chistoe Nebo members."

"At first glance, the case imposed on Goryanoy is full of dead ends - the tattooed Shkaf, his fighters with bar codes, the strange behavior of Barin, the equally strange death of those Svobodovites at the halt. However, there is one clear trace - the Dolgovites captured the marked Svobodovite commander and were going to deliver him to Agroprom. Let's start with that."

"Finally, a worthwhile clue. I'm sure Goryan was careful with his words and didn't say anything that could give a "direct lead", but there is one "but" - he has no idea what resources an ordinary stalker might have. First, the leader of "Clear Sky" nicknamed Hawk... It seems that even this "pseudonym" was heard by very few in the Zone. And they knew it for too short a time to figure out what was what. A headache for Griff and his vaunted analysts. Secondly, the mercenary Scar. Never heard of this stalker, I'll have to ask Czech about him. Thirdly, "Freedom" and, possibly, "Duty" know something about some "legacy" of Clear Sky. Obviously, these are the remains of the whole, otherwise the bird base would have been gutted long ago. Fourthly, the base – it does exist. However, the question remains open – is it really empty, or are the stalkers’ tales about the clan rushing north in full force – nonsense, not worth a dime. The fulfillment of one of the conditions of the order – about the liquidation of the remaining members of the Clear Sky group – depends on this. It may well be that both Duty and Freedom have different pieces of the puzzle, and with the right skill they can be put together, but there is no point in forcing information out of Goryan and his henchmen now. With a skillful approach, he will lay everything out himself, and his people will help with fire support if necessary. That leaves Duty, and we will start with it."

"Anomalies are the key. Two Angels are enough to check. While Nikolai Vasilyevich tests object #1, Lodygin will take up a position at Pepelishche. Object #2 will also be under observation." "We still couldn't get the name out of him. Shevchuk needed it for some reason, but everything is clear to me anyway. Equipment, personal notes - and it's clear as day that this is a ChistoNebovets. Strong, bitch, like flint. By the time they took him, he'd taken out two of ours. And he didn't crack. Shevchuk will answer for this. And the ChistoNebovets... He's already answered."

"Black angels are large anomalous formations of a mixed type. Initial measurements show extremely high gravitational and psi-activity. Four such formations have been registered. The first contact was registered a month and a half ago, three out of five in the group of free stalkers from Cordon died. According to information from the survivors, the anomaly literally split the bodies into molecules, they simply disappeared into thin air. This allows us to suggest the possibility of not direct destruction, but teleportation in space - anomalies with a similar type of action, although rare, are encountered in the Zone." "The dimensions are large, the anomalous focus itself is up to 10 meters in diameter, the zone of active influence is up to 50 meters. Effects are possible that arise from similar psi-influence from anomalies such as "Scar", or psi-zones, or a short-term psi-field during emission. During the entire period of existence, no formation of artifacts has been registered. However, this cannot unambiguously indicate the absence of the possibility of the formation of art formations by the Black Angels." "It has been established that the said anomalies are prone to displacement. The total number remains unchanged, but the location of each of the Black Angels changes periodically. Based on observations, it can be assumed that the displacements are not related to emissions. If such a hypothesis is confirmed, it can be confidently stated that there is another source of influence on the Black Angels, different from the emissions of anomalous energy in the Center of the Zone. The influence of emissions on anomalies within the Zone has been practically unstudied; only general principles have been established through actual observation, so such a hypothesis, along with others, requires clarification and research. A more detailed study is impossible due to the lack of necessary equipment and skills. A full-fledged research team headed by a level "A" specialist is required to conduct a full range of measurements and experimental sampling of substances."

"I still don't understand their plan. If they wanted to establish dominance over all these southern territories, they would have done it long ago. And neither Duty nor Freedom would have been able to oppose them. And so - incomprehensible lumps-mice. As if they are really playing with us and with them. Or they are waiting for me to lead them to their intended goal and lay everything out on a silver platter. Heh, they won't wait. Although either Griff miscalculated, or the ones who set up those brigades are just geniuses and brought everything to this outcome. Do they really not feel sorry for their people, whom we are piling up here? No, there must have been some kind of catch here, which I am not yet able to figure out. Rumor has it that everything was going too smoothly. Who is their observer and coordinator? That is another question. That ambush was not just like that - they really were waiting for me, but... Why couldn't they kill me then, there were opportunities and more than one... Okay, let's try to play by their own rules. This prisoner will still be useful to me. Let's try to play this game to its logical conclusion. It remains unclear who killed those Svoboda members. Duty, mercenaries - who? Maybe it was a falsification to confuse everything completely, or to send me on a false trail... Freaks, pretending to be white and fluffy cuties. No, it's not that simple, Red, not everyone... "

“So, gentlemen, the trap is set. Everyone has taken their places in the auditorium - we are waiting for the red-finned barrel organ to come out. The main thing now is that this ambush is not burned ahead of time. We will have to eliminate their patrols. That means another ten minutes before the operation begins. They have already intercepted the messages. So, that means they should have already set out. By the time they arrive, by the time they take up positions - another three minutes. I think I will have time... And the Svoboda members still need to be checked... They definitely need to be checked. They have played the victim too skillfully, positioning themselves right under the assDebt and mercenaries. And somehow suspiciously no one touches their main group. Suspicious, too suspicious..."

"Everything worked like a British motherfucker... The charges didn't kill everyone at once, I had to finish them off. No big deal, at least the bastards suffered. Now it's time to play the last ace up my sleeve. Funny, but I didn't have to wait for it. That means the drug is working fine. Let's see how long I can keep it under my control. Even if it doesn't work out, their last point of deployment will be in close proximity for a strike. I don't think they're coordinating there. Their dispatcher isn't such a fool as to act so obviously. Let's see how it ends. I think this is the last search group operating here. And what's next, we'll see. We need to get to the Clear Sky base at any cost. Let's go to the end."

"This fucking psychotropic drug didn't work as it should. Either the dose was too small, or the mercenary wasn't a weakling. I still couldn't get him to do what I was counting on. I had to kill him. However, this didn't affect the execution of the operation by and large. The southern farmstead has been cleared, the final documents have been found. It's time to report to the customer. However, even after studying the materials found on the mercenary squad commanders, it was not possible to establish who exactly they were working for. A syndicate? Unlikely, but not pointless - betting everything on me alone is stupid. But if this structure is capable of organizing several squads of fighters, equipped to the highest standard, and delivering them to the Swamps - why would they need me? Another possible option is the Glock. Neither Czech nor I were able to find out anything about this stalker. Yes, we heard, yes, we dealt with him a couple of times, and that's it. This stalker is not simple, oh, not simple. This version is supported by the fact that an ambush was organized during the meeting with Sedy, where this Glock figured. There were also measurements there. However, it doesn't matter who they work for. Let Grif and his analysts rack their brains over that. The problem is that the mercenaries turned out to be much more productive than the Dolgovites, and were able to get very close to solving the mystery of "Clear Sky". I am one step behind them, although I have eliminated the main strike forces. Judging by the details, there were many more people "quartered" at the southern farmstead than during the assault. This means that some of the mercenaries had already left by that time. Where, why, and how to look for them now - that is the question. »

“The operation using a planted mercenary went off without a hitch. He played his role superbly and played the role of a suicide bomber. Good. Very good. The last search party of competitors has been destroyed. So we report to the intermediary and continue the operation. However, even after studying the materials found on the commanders of the mercenary squads, it was not possible to establish who exactly they were working for. A syndicate? Unlikely, but not pointless - betting everything on just me alone is stupid. But if this structure is capable of organizing several squads of fighters, equipped to the highest standard, and delivering them to the Swamps - why would they need me? Another possible option is Glock. Neither I nor Czech could find out anything about this stalker. Yes, we heard, yes, we dealt with him a couple of times, and that's it. This stalker is not simple, oh, not simple. The fact that an ambush was organized during a meeting with Sedy, where this Glock figured, speaks in favor of this version. There were measurements there too. However, it doesn't matter who they work for. Let Grif and his analysts rack their brains over that. The problem is that the mercenaries turned out to be much more productive than the Dolgovites, and were able to get very close to solving the mystery of "Clear Sky". I am one step behind them, although I have eliminated the main strike forces. Judging by the details, there were many more people "quartered" at the southern farmstead than during the assault. This means that some of the mercenaries had already left by that time. Where, why, and how to look for them now - that is the question. »

"I finally managed to find this lab. What a horror. A very godforsaken place. It's just not clear how exactly it could have become like this in just a couple of weeks. Well, now it looks like everything is falling into place. Clear Sky uses this collector for initial experiments, but the main research is taking place in the Valley of Shadows. The mercenaries managed to figure out its location just like the Black Angels. Damn it, this Black Angel. He's confusing all our cards,transferring the game to a completely different plane. I still have no idea who they are and why they are here. But they are determined, that's a fact. Another party has interfered in the division of the Clear Sky inheritance. Funny. However, they left the lab in a hurry. One of their people died before I found them, which means... Although no, wait. This doesn't mean anything. He could have simply run into an anomaly or caught a stray bullet. It's unlikely that they dragged him wounded specifically to this place. Think Red, think... The mercenaries went the same way and came to the surface at the base. They were one step ahead of you, just one. But they weren't interested in the information you managed to find here. Or were they not looking for it? And why weren't the Angels looking for it? Damn, and why didn't they kill this Clear Sky man? There are too many of these questions. I'll find out everything at the base, it won't be long... This is not what I wanted to hear from Kitov when I interrogated him. Yes, and before that I knew perfectly well that the Syndicate is not a boarding house for noble girls, and that there are some higher powers in the Zone that have taken upon themselves the right to decide the fates of people who, willingly or unwillingly, fell into the trap of the Zone. But... I am a killer, a cold-blooded assassin for hire, who carries out orders. A killer, but not a butcher. And therefore I cannot allow these capsules to fall into the hands of the Griff or the minions of the O-Consciousness. The Zone is a personal hell on earth for people like me, and this hell must not enter the lives and destinies of ordinary people who have not stained themselves with blood and live by virtue. To hell with the rules. To hell with the Code. I'm not a mercenary anymore. My inner voice back then, in the seedy bar of Staroselye, sang along with all the chorales, but not in alarm, but in delight. The delight of future redemption. I didn't hear it then. I didn't understand. Now everything has changed. Life leads us to death in different ways. And even for those like me, a soldier of fortune who lives only for today, there is a choice. Choice. There. Always.»

“The debt and the O-Consciousness mercenaries have been virtually completely neutralized—perhaps there are still a few scattered units left, but that’s unlikely. All the information they managed to find is now with me. Let’s begin deciphering the data. Somewhere, information about the existence and location of the route to the Clear Sky base will surface. The last strike force of Clear Sky, and its commander—a stalker nicknamed Cold. This is one of the few that Griff mentioned in advance, and even provided a photo of. Even before the final dash of most of the group to the north, to the Chernobyl Nuclear Power Plant, Cold was listed as a local merchant and quartermaster for the “food part.” Whatever one may say, the group could not exist in complete isolation, and Cold acted as an intermediary.between stalker traders and "Clear Sky". Naturally, under cover - an ordinary lone stalker, sometimes selling artifacts, buying food and other everyday goods. The syndicate singled him out from the crowd thanks to the analysis of purchase volumes. Local traders do not get along with each other, and even more so do not report to each other - who sold how much and what amounts they received. Meanwhile, Kholod with a couple of assistants bought the necessary goods within reasonable limits and without arousing suspicion, but at the same time - from almost every trader. Thus, a fairly impressive volume was accumulated, which supplemented Suslov's supplies from outside the Perimeter. Kholod, apparently, did not take part in the dash to the north and was left in charge, gathered a punitive strike force from the remnants of the group, led it and began his sabotage activities. I doubt that this was a banal revenge on everyone and everything. No, most likely he pursued a simple and logical goal - to save what was left of the group, to preserve the legacy of Clear Sky, to wait for the return of comrades and clansmen from the north. Kholod and Co. could have kept quiet, but sooner or later the path to their secret base would have been found. That is why they were forced to make sorties and attacks on those who managed to get too close to them. But there is a limit to everything. For Kholod and his teammates, it came today."
